title: Post-call: ThermoGen private deployment follow-ups (air-gapped ops & HW)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Sutherland <tom.s@thermogen.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 20 Jun 2027 15:04:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap & next steps — ThermoGen private deployment (air-gapped)

Tom — thanks for taking the call earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment before the technical deep dive: 

Summary of your deployment intent
- Goal: run inference for proprietary genomic pipelines inside an air-gapped cluster (no outbound internet).
- Target throughput: ~600 requests/min peak, typical sequence lengths ~1k tokens.
- RPO/RTO: batch-style inference acceptable; 99th-pct latency target 450ms for interactive endpoints.

Open qualification items (we discussed on call):
1) Hardware ownership: ThermoGen procurement vs Redwood-supplied appliance.
2) Upgrade model: offline maintenance windows and how we deliver patches (USB/OTA alternative).
3) Network topology: where model artifacts and checkpoint images will be staged; need network diagram.
4) Data handling: KMS/HSM integrations and audit log retention.

Proposed immediate next steps
- I will loop in our ops lead (Diego) and solutions engineer (Monica) — they’ll propose a minimal hardware profile and offline patch MOP.
- Please share your network diagram and SOC artifact (if available) before the deep-dive: upload to the Drive link below or reply to this thread.
- Schedule a 60-minute technical deep dive (we’re proposing Wed 6/23 or Thu 6/24 in the morning PT).

Attachments in this thread: estimate_RDW-4521.xlsx (initial pricing ballpark).

If that list looks right, please reply with preferred deep-dive slot and an initial decision on procurement (ThermoGen buys HW vs Redwood ships appliance).

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[attachment: estimate_RDW-4521.xlsx (Excel, 34 KB)]


From: Tom Sutherland <tom.s@thermogen.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Ng <l.ng@thermogen.com>
Date: Sun, 20 Jun 2027 16:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & next steps — ThermoGen private deployment (air-gapped)

Ben — thanks for the clear recap. A few clarifications from our side: 

1) Procurement: we prefer to purchase hardware through our vendors (capital spend). Can Redwood provide a validated BOM and equivalently supported parts list so we can route through procurement?

2) Upgrades: we need a fully offline upgrade path. No outbound connectivity permitted from the cluster except to a physically-connected jump host inside our DMZ during scheduled windows. Are USB-signed images or offline package bundles supported? What’s the rollback story?

3) Network diagram: Laura is attaching an initial internal diagram (sanitized) — it's on our side but I can upload to the Drive link.

4) Security evidence: we have a SOC2 readiness pack but not a full SOC2 report. Can Redwood accept scoped evidence in lieu of a full report for the initial procurement?

Deep-dive: Thu 6/24 9:00 PT works for me.

Thanks,
Tom
Tom Sutherland
Director, Infrastructure — ThermoGen
[attachment: thermogen_network_diagram_v2.pdf (PDF, 120 KB)]


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Sutherland <tom.s@thermogen.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>, Laura Ng <l.ng@thermogen.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & next steps — ThermoGen private deployment (air-gapped)

Appreciate the quick reply, Tom — answers inline and next steps below.

1) Validated BOM: Monica will attach a validated BOM and supported parts list (we maintain an "RDX-validated" components table that procurement can use). If you prefer to run procurement, we’ll mark required parts and firmware versions.

2) Offline upgrades & rollback: Yes — we support signed offline bundles. Typical flow we recommend:
- We generate a signed update bundle (image + manifest + signature).
- ThermoGen transfers bundle via encrypted USB or to an internal jump host in the DMZ.
- On acceptance, ops apply bundle; system performs a snapshot-based install and can roll back to previous snapshot within 15 minutes.
We’ll include an offline MOP and sample USB verification checklist in the deep-dive.

3) Network diagram — received, thanks. We’ll review before the call.

4) SOC evidence — scoped artifacts are fine for the procurement phase. For final contract we’ll need standard compliance attestations; we can accept an internal SOC2 readiness pack to start.

Action items before Thu 6/24 09:00 PT deep-dive:
- Monica: validated BOM + minimal HW profile + attachment of redwood_private_hw_requirements_v1.pdf.
- Diego: offline upgrade MOP draft + runbook link.
- ThermoGen: confirm procurement direction and upload any site access constraints.

I’ve tentatively scheduled the deep-dive for Thu 6/24 09:00 PT (calendar invite to follow).

Best,
Ben


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Tom Sutherland <tom.s@thermogen.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>, Laura Ng <l.ng@thermogen.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood — minimal hardware profile & air-gap checklist (draft)

Tom / team — attaching our draft minimal hardware profile and an air-gap checklist. Highlights: 

Minimal profile (validated):
- 8x A100-style GPUs (40GB) or 12x T4 equivalence for mixed workloads.
- 2x redundant NICs to the DMZ jump host; isolated management network for KVM/ILO.
- 2x NVMe arrays (RAID1) for model cache + separate persistent storage for datasets.
- HSM/KMS edge appliance supported via offline import of keys (bring-your-own-HSM supported).

Air-gap & offline upgrade notes:
- Release bundles are cryptographically signed; verification uses an offline key validation tool (we’ll provide).
- Upgrade performs block-level snapshot before install; rollback available if health checks fail.
- Recommended maintenance window: 2 hours per node for major upgrades; 30–45 minutes for minor/patch releases.

Actionable ask: please confirm whether ThermoGen will manage hardware procurement (we’ll supply exact SKUs) or prefer Redwood-supplied appliances. If ThermoGen buys, share procurement vendor contact and preferred lead time (typical delivery 6–10 weeks for validated SKUs).

Attachment: [attachment: redwood_private_hw_requirements_v1.pdf (PDF, 210 KB)]

—Monica
Monica Patel
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Tom Sutherland <tom.s@thermogen.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>, Laura Ng <l.ng@thermogen.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 10:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood — minimal hardware profile & air-gap checklist (draft)

Monica / Ben — this is excellent, thanks. A couple of quick confirmations: 

• Procurement: ThermoGen will purchase hardware. Procurement team prefers a single-line SKU list we can attach to the PO; please provide SKUs and recommended vendor (we use CDW and Ingram for procurement).
• Maintenance windows: 2-hour windows are acceptable; we tend to do rolling upgrades during our weekly maintenance window Fri 02:00–06:00 PT.
• Offline verification: we’ll test signed bundles on a staging node first. Please include the verification tool + sample invocation in the runbook.

I’ve uploaded an initial SOC evidence pack to the Drive link (thermogen_soc2_readiness.pdf). I’ll accept the calendar invite for Thu 6/24 09:00 PT. Looking forward to the deep-dive.

Tom

[attachment: thermogen_soc2_readiness.pdf (PDF, 470 KB)]

title: Customer RFP: Technical Account Manager coverage, escalation flow, and incident comms
preview:
From: Evan Liu <evan.liu@luma.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Menon <priya.m@luma.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: RFP follow-up: TAM coverage, escalation expectations

Hi Aditya,

Thanks for the quick intro call last week. As part of our procurement packet we're consolidating answers for the support & TAM section of the RFP. Can you provide a short, itemized response for Luma that covers the following:?

- Dedicated TAM coverage model (hours / SLA for email, phone, P1/P2)
- On-call / after-hours escalation path and guaranteed response windows
- Roster: primary/on-call shifts and names (we can accept role placeholders for now)
- Incident communications workflow (who we hear from at what cadence and what channels)
- Evidence: SOC 2 report availability, audit logs, and retention policy summary

We have a target to submit the RFP by EOD Tuesday (6/22). If you can send a consolidated document or a quick email summary, that would be ideal.

Best,
Evan
Evan Liu
Head of SRE, Luma Financial

--
Attachment: Luma_RFP_Template_v3.docx (referenced, not sent yet)


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: RFP follow-up: TAM coverage, escalation expectations

Team — forwarding Luma's questions. They want a concise, bulletized answer they can paste into the procurement packet. Deadline EOD Tue 6/22.

Neha: can you draft the TAM coverage (hours, SLA windows, P1/P2 response times)?
Marissa: can you assemble the roster CSV (role placeholders ok) and the escalation diagram?
Ben: flag anything commercial (reserved hours / retainer language) we should call out so procurement doesn't misinterpret.

I'll own the consolidated PDF and the Drive link. Please add answers in-line on this thread so I can collate.

Thanks — Aditya

On Mon, Jun 14, 2027 at 10:12 AM Evan Liu <evan.liu@luma.com> wrote:
> [See above]


From: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 09:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: RFP follow-up: TAM coverage, escalation expectations

AD: Draft answers below — keeping these short so procurement can paste directly. Marissa, I'll reference the roster file for names. Ben, I flagged commercial notes with [COMM] tags.

1) TAM coverage model:
- Primary TAM for Luma (Dedicated): 40 hours/week standard engagement + 8 hours/week strategic planning (retainer).
- Email/Portal response: P1 (sev/production down) — initial ack within 30 minutes, phone/video response within 2 hours; P2 (major impact) — ack within 2 hours, response within 6 hours.
- Business hours: 07:00–19:00 PT (Mon–Fri). After-hours coverage is on-call rotation for P1 only.
- [COMM] Retainer covers up to 4 on-call P1 responses per month; additional P1 incidents billed at T&M unless a SLA addendum is signed.

2) On-call & escalation path (summary):
- Level 0: Luma first responder (customer ops) -> Level 1: Redwood TAM (primary) -> Level 2: Redwood Platform Infra & Eng on-call -> Level 3: Engineering escalation (swarm) / Executive paging if >4 hours impact.
- Escalation windows and primary contacts are included in the roster CSV (see attachment).

3) Roster (placeholder roles allowed): attached CSV contains: role, name (placeholder), on-call phone, escalation order, timezone.

4) Incident communications workflow:
- For P1 incidents: phone alert to TAM within 15 minutes of detection, 15-min status updates for first hour, then 30-min cadence until resolution. Postmortem delivered within 72 hours; RCA and action plan within 10 business days.
- For P2: status email every 2 hours during business hours; summary and RCA within 7 business days.

5) Evidence & retention:
- SOC 2 Type II report: available under NDA; we can provide the report and bridge letter.
- Audit logs: retention 365 days by default; can be extended via contract.
- Data retention / export: customers may request full exports; we maintain sandboxed retention controls per account.

Let me know if you'd like me to tighten timing language or remove the commercial callouts before final.

-Neha

--
Attachment referenced: redwood-support-roster-2027.csv


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 16 Jun 2027 08:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: RFP follow-up: TAM coverage, escalation expectations

Team — I created two artifacts: 1) tam-escalation-diagram.png (simple flowchart for procurement), 2) Luma-RFP-TAM-Answers.pdf which integrates Neha's bullets and the roster CSV.

Quick notes:
- I normalized time windows to PT as requested; included explicit phone escalation numbers (placeholder roles) so Luma has something to ingest.
- For SOC 2 we added the standard NDA line and an option to provide a redacted report directly to security.

I'll attach the artifacts to the draft Drive doc; Aditya, ping me if you want stronger SLA commitments called out — I erred conservative to avoid contractual exposure.

-Marissa

Attachments: tam-escalation-diagram.png, Luma-RFP-TAM-Answers.pdf

To: Evan Liu <evan.liu@luma.com>, Priya Menon <priya.m@luma.com>

Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 08:05:00 -07:00

Hi Evan / Priya — please find our consolidated responses attached and a short summary below (the PDF and roster CSV are linked in Drive). Happy to hop on a 30-min call if you want to walk through any line items.

Summary highlights:
- Dedicated TAM model: 40h/week primary, on-call rotation for P1 after-hours; P1 ack within 30 minutes, phone response within 2 hours.
- Incident comms: 15-min updates for the first hour on P1; postmortem within 72 hours, RCA & action plan in 10 business days.
- Evidence: SOC 2 Type II available under NDA; audit logs retained 365 days by default.
- Commercial note: retainer covers up to 4 after-hours P1s/mo; additional incidents billed at T&M unless an SLA addendum is executed.

Drive links: https://drive.redwood.com/rdw/luma-rfp-answers-2027
DocuSign: DOCUSIGN-REQ-44921 (if procurement requires signatures)

Thanks — looking forward to your feedback; we can tighten SLA language if needed for contracting.

-Aditya

From: Evan Liu <evan.liu@luma.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Menon <priya.m@luma.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Customer RFP: Technical Account Manager coverage, escalation flow, and incident comms

Aditya / team — this is great, thank you. The consolidated PDF and roster give procurement what they need. A few quick clarifications we might ask procurement to accept:

- Confirming: postmortem within 72 hours is fine; RCA in 10 business days accepted.
- Could you add a short sentence that on-call rotations will include regional overlap (PT and ET) for primary coverage?
- We'll sign the NDA for SOC 2 and follow via DocuSign if required.

I'll flag internally that we may request a slightly expanded log retention window for one regulated workload — will follow up if that becomes a requirement.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Best,
Evan

title: Post-demo: inference path checklist & unit-econ test plan
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Morgan <alex.morgan@bluepointhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 20 Jun 2027 15:02:00 +0000
Subject: Post-demo: inference path checklist & unit-econ test plan

Hi Alex —

Thanks again for carving out time today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned: we heard that latency at p95, predictable per-request unit economics, and a safe private deployment option are the top priorities. On the call we ran through how Redwood compares vs a couple of hyperscalers and a self-host baseline; below is a pragmatic set of follow-up tests we recommend to validate cost and performance for your workload.

Summary of high-level differentiators we discussed:
- Redwood: unified runtime across hosted/dedicated/private, automatic prefix/KV caching, transparent per-route cost breakdowns. Good fit if you need a predictable SLA and clear cost controls.
- Hyperscaler A: deep infra discounts but less inference-specific batching & KV cache optimizations out-of-the-box.
- Self-host (optimized): lowest raw per-token infra cost but higher ops burden and variable reliability.

Proposed test matrix (short):
1) Unit-econ run (1M tokens simulated per variant)
   - Config: average prompt 300 tokens, average completion 200 tokens (total 500 tokens/request); batch sizes: 1, 8, 32; concurrency profile: 50 sustained rps peak window; cache scenarios: 0%, 30%, 60% prefix hit.
   - Measure: cost per 1M tokens, p50/p95 latency, throughput (tokens/s) and error/retry rates.

2) Latency SLO verification (90-minute sustained run at 50rps), measure p95 and tail behaviors.

3) Quantization & model variant check: FP16 vs int8-quantized variant to validate quality regression and cost delta.

Preliminary unit economics (back-of-envelope assumptions we discussed):
- Redwood hosted (standard model): $9.50 per 1M tokens (assumes 30% batching efficiency and 0% cache hits).
- Hyperscaler A managed endpoint: $15.00 per 1M tokens (no inference-specific KV cache assumed).
- Hyperscaler B (spot-managed): ~$11.00 per 1M tokens (higher variability).
- On-prem optimized (amortized infra + ops): ~$4.50 per 1M tokens (excludes ops headcount).

Note: cache hit rate materially shifts unit cost. Example: at Redwood, a 30% prefix cache hit reduces effective billed tokens by ~30% for repeated contexts, bringing per-1M to approx $6.65 in our model. Batching also compounds savings — moving average batch from 1 to 32 can cut per-token kernel overheads by ~20-30% depending on sequence length distribution. We'll include formulas in the CSV.

Attachments: bluepoint-unit-econ-proposal.csv (test matrix + cost calc), call-recording-20270620.mp4 (30m).

Next steps / asks for BluePoint:
- Please confirm the sample corpus you want us to use for the 1M-token run (anonymized traces are fine).
- Confirm whether you want us to include your private-diff model variant (we can stage a private deployment for the test if needed).
- Would you like Redwood to run the tests (recommended) or share configs so your infra team can run the same matrix?

If you confirm today we can schedule the Redwood-run tests for next Tue/Wed and share a first-pass report within 48 hours after the runs.

Best,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference
Attachment: bluepoint-unit-econ-proposal.csv (CSV, 14KB)
Attachment: call-recording-20270620.mp4 (MP4, 36MB)

From: Alex Morgan <alex.morgan@bluepointhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 20 Jun 2027 17:10:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Post-demo: inference path checklist & unit-econ test plan

Thanks Ben — this is helpful and lines up with what we need. A couple quick clarifications before we commit to Redwood-run tests:

- For the 1M token run, what exact sequence length distribution are you assuming? Our traffic is skewed: 70% short (<=128 tokens) and 30% long (>=800 tokens) completions.
- For batching: can you confirm how you map request-level batching to our latency SLOs? We need p95 < 350ms for short requests.
- Can you include the Hyperscaler A (us-east managed) pricing sheet and the config you used to estimate their $15/1M? We want the apples-to-apples comparison.

We can send an anonymized sample corpus tonight — I'll share a Drive link. If Redwood can run the tests, please include per-route and per-token cost breakdowns and the raw logs.

Also, we prefer Redwood to run the tests so we can avoid ops time on our side.

Alex
Director, ML Platforms
BluePoint Health

> (original message)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Morgan <alex.morgan@bluepointhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 20 Jun 2027 18:20:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Post-demo: inference path checklist & unit-econ test plan

Alex — great questions, thanks. Short answers and concrete test configs below.

Sequence-length distribution (acknowledged): we'll run the 70/30 split you listed and include a second sweep that is 100% short to validate p95 for the low-latency path.

Batching -> latency mapping:
- Short requests (<=128 tokens): we will prioritize low-latency path: target batch size = 1-4, dynamic batching enabled with 80ms max wait window, expected p95 ~250-320ms in Redwood hosted per our prior runs.
- Long requests: will leverage larger batches (8-32) to maximize throughput and reduce per-token kernel overhead.

Hyperscaler assumptions we used for the $15/1M estimate (apples-to-apples):
- Managed endpoint using standard GPU (A100 equivalent), no KV cache, identical model architecture, 24/7 uptime pricing, includes 20% managed infra premium.
- We will attach the pricing sheet to the run report for transparency.

Proposed final test matrix (we'll run if you confirm):
- Workloads: your 70/30 split + 100% short sweep.
- Batch sizes: 1, 8, 32.
- Cache hit rates: 0%, 30% (simulate repeated prompts), 60% (for high reuse workflows).
- Variants: FP16, int8-quantized.
- Metrics: cost/1M, p50/p95/p99 latency, tokens/s, percent of requests meeting SLO, and quality deltas (for quantization).

I'll loop Marissa and Karthik to schedule infra and attach an initial estimate to the HubSpot deal. Expect a dedicated run environment and a link to raw logs.

Ben

Redwood Inference

- Managed endpoint using standard GPU (A100 equivalent), no KV cache, identical model architecture, 24/7 uptime pricing, includes 20% managed infra premium.
- We will attach the pricing sheet to the run report for transparency.

Proposed final test matrix (we'll run if you confirm):
- Workloads: your 70/30 split + 100% short sweep.
- Batch sizes: 1, 8, 32.
- Cache hit rates: 0%, 30% (simulate repeated prompts), 60% (for high reuse workflows).
- Variants: FP16, int8-quantized.
- Metrics: cost/1M, p50/p95/p99 latency, tokens/s, percent of requests meeting SLO, and quality deltas (for quantization).

I'll loop Marissa and Karthik to schedule infra and attach an initial estimate to the HubSpot deal. Expect a dedicated run environment and a link to raw logs.

Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference

title: Offer syndication: SKU gating and cloud-specific pricing constraints
preview:
From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-13T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Offer syndication: SKU gating and cloud-specific pricing constraints

Team —

Attaching the first cut of the SKU matrix for marketplace listings (redwood-sku-proposal-v1.xlsx). Goal is to create a single offer that can be syndicated to AWS, Azure, and GCP but with cloud-specific gating and packaging where required. High-level thinking:

- Build three canonical SKUs: Core (consumption-friendly, metered), Scale (reserved + commit), Enterprise (annual with premium support).
- Add-ons: private VPC deployment, HIPAA package, EDU/nonprofit bundle as separate line items.
- For Azure we should present a listed price that meets co-sell incentive thresholds; for AWS we may need to split billing (Marketplace metered vs. Reserved contracts) to preserve channel credits; GCP wants a trial-first flow which impacts how we price the Core SKU.

Open questions I want input on:
1) Minimum listed price per cloud to qualify for co-sell incentives (Ben to check AWS thresholds).
2) Region gating for EU/UK data — do we list a separate EU SKU or use regional pricing overrides?
3) How to handle billing modality differences (metered vs. subscription) without creating too many SKUs.

Please review the attached and flag anything that would break intake on the partner portals. I suggest we lock decisions by EOD next Friday so Soojin can start intake packets.

Thanks —
Olga
Director, GTM Partnerships
Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood-sku-proposal-v1.xlsx


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-14T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Offer syndication: SKU gating and cloud-specific pricing constraints

On Sun, Jun 13, 2027 at 9:12 AM Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Attaching the first cut of the SKU matrix for marketplace listings...

Quick notes after a pass on the spreadsheet:

- AWS co-sell: historically AWS co-sell incentives kick in for transactable offers >= $10k list price or for ARR equivalent on reserved/committed deals. We should confirm the current partner program threshold; it moves. If we can get the Scale SKU at $12k+ list it helps.
- Azure: co-sell requires explicit metadata (offer category, SKUs mapped to Metered vs. SaaS subscription), and there is a minimum transactable price for marketplace incentives — usually higher than AWS. Samira (we should confirm) but assume $15k+.
- GCP: requires a free trial/first-class trial SKU to be present to be listed in their catalog. That means the Core SKU should be trialable with a usage cap.
- Region gating: prefer a single SKU with regional price overrides in the matrix rather than separate SKUs unless legal requires distinct T&Cs for EU.

I flagged a few pricing examples in column D of the spreadsheet. Also added a column for 'co-sell friendliness' per cloud.

Ben
Director, Cloud GTM
Redwood Inference


From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <s.patel@azuremarketplace.com>, Liam O'Connell <liam.oconnell@gcp-partners.com>, Jenna Li <jenna.li@aws-partners.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-15T14:23:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Offer syndication: SKU gating and cloud-specific pricing constraints

Hi Samira / Liam / Jenna —

Forwarding our internal SKU draft and initial thoughts from Ben and Olga (attached). We're aligning offers for the three clouds and want to validate two things before we finalize:

1) Minimum transactable/listed price per cloud to trigger co-sell incentives or co-sell routing.
2) Required SKU-level metadata and acceptable billing modalities (metered vs. subscription) per cloud — any restrictions on packaging multiple add-ons as separate SKU lines?

Quick context: we propose three canonical SKUs (Core, Scale, Enterprise) + separate add-ons. We prefer to keep the published SKU count low and use price/metadata overrides per cloud and region. If a cloud requires separate SKUs to qualify for incentives, we need that call now so we don't over-index on SKU proliferation.

I've attached our co-sell checklist to show intake items we can supply. Could you confirm the gating rules and any region-specific constraints (EU/UK/Germany) you enforce?

Thanks —
Soojin
Partner Ops, Redwood Inference
Attachments: co-sell-checklist.pdf


From: Samira Patel <s.patel@azuremarketplace.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
Cc: Liam O'Connell <liam.oconnell@gcp-partners.com>
Date: 2027-06-16T11:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Offer syndication: SKU gating and cloud-specific pricing constraints

Thanks for the packet, Soojin —

High level from Azure Marketplace side:

- Co-sell qualifying offers typically need to be transactable in the Marketplace and have a clear published price (subscriptions or BYOL+marketplace metering are OK but metadata must reflect pricing model). Our current incentive floor for co-sell routing is usually $15k list annualized, but there are program levers for strategic ISVs — we can request an exception if you have a committed pipeline.
- Trial-first flow is supported but it must map to a SKU with a limited duration (e.g., 30 days) and the trial SKU needs to be flagged in the offer metadata.
- For EU/UK region gating: if you're offering a separate legal entity or different data residency terms you should publish an EU-specific offer; otherwise price overrides per region are sufficient. Germany has additional compliance flags for certain regulated workloads.

Next steps I recommend:
1) Provide a sample JSON metadata for each SKU (title, billing model, trial flag, list price, eligibility tags) so we can pre-validate in our portal.
2) Mark any customers in your pipeline that would make the $15k floor obvious — that helps for co-sell uplift.

I'll loop in the marketplace intake specialist if you want us to pre-approve the metadata.

Best —
Samira Patel
Principal Partner Manager, Azure Marketplace
Microsoft Corporation


From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <s.patel@azuremarketplace.com>, Liam O'Connell <liam.oconnell@gcp-partners.com>, Jenna Li <jenna.li@aws-partners.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-20T16:42:00-07:00
Subject: Finalized approach and next steps: SKU matrix + intake artifacts

Thanks for the quick responses and clarifications. Summary of decisions and actions (locking this week):

- SKU model: Core (trialable, metered), Scale (transactable subscription, target list $12k-$18k depending on cloud), Enterprise (annual, custom pricing).
- Azure: treat Scale as $15k list for co-sell readiness; include a 30-day trial flagged in metadata for Core.
- AWS: keep Scale at $12k list but include a reserved billing mapping so field sellers can convert to committed deals; Jenna, we'll need a note on AWS co-sell intake for reserved mapping.
- GCP: require a Core trial SKU; finalize trial usage caps for intake.
- Regions: we will publish a single global SKU set with regional price overrides; for EU-specific legal differences we will publish a separate EU offer if legal flags arise.

Attached is the final_sku_matrix_20270620.xlsx with per-cloud columns filled and the HubSpot opportunity ID included. Next immediate asks:
1) Samira/Jenna/Liam — if you can pre-validate the attached metadata (JSON examples are in the Drive link) that would shorten intake time.
2) Ben/Marissa — prepare a short intake pack for the field (1-pager + pricing cheat sheet) by EOD Wednesday.
3) Soojin — start the formal intake submissions for Azure and AWS next Monday; GCP trailboarding to follow once trial caps are agreed.

Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/sku-master-2027
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/company/890123/opportunity/HP-2027-MARKET-42
DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/sign/abc123-marketplace-co-sell

I'll schedule a 30-minute follow-up next week to confirm intake status.

—Olga
Director, GTM Partnerships
Redwood Inference

title: Weekly leadership signalcheck + QBR prep: decisions & handoffs
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-06-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Weekly leadership signalcheck + QBR prep: decisions & handoffs

Team — weekly snapshot ahead of QBR week. Short, actionable. Please read and flag any major blockers by EOD Wed.

Wins
- Hosted API latency improvements: p95 reduced from 210ms -> 165ms after kernel swap; customer A saw 12% cost reduction in June.
- Redwood Optimize pilot with Beta customers (3) delivered batching/caching recommendations that reduced token spend by ~18% on average.
- Dedicated onboarding flow: first dedicated customer went live with zero data leaks and full audit logs.

Risks / Signals
1) Capacity squeeze for northbound requests during peak (expected week-of-6/28) — current spare capacity forecast down to ~8% without burst.
2) Recent model regression in reranking tests (latent quality drop on a specific prompt cohort) — investigation underway (Research + Models).
3) QBR pack for the Enterprise segment is missing final SLAs and pricing ask from Finance.

Asks / Decisions (high priority)
1) Capacity commit: request approval to provision a short-term 4-week GPU burst pool (cost estimate attached) to cover 6/28–7/25 traffic. Owner: Jonas. Needed by Fri EOD for procurement.
2) Pricing/terms: confirm margin floor for enterprise renewals so Sales can finalize quotes to Acme by 6/24. Owner: Laura + Finance.
3) QBR runbook: review attached runbook.xlsx and flag gaps; need signoff on incident handoff and post-mortem cadence. Owner: Karthik.
4) Dry-run: propose a 45-minute exec dry-run on Tue 6/22 10:30–11:15 PT — please confirm availability via calendar poll (link below).

Attachments: QBR_onepager.pdf (one-pager for execs), runbook.xlsx (ops runbook stub). Drive links below.

Thanks — will consolidate replies and publish an updated pack by 6/19 5pm.

—
Ava Chen
VP, Product
Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.ai

To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>

Thanks Ava — quick reactions:
- Capacity: I support the burst pool for the 4-week window if Finance signs the run rate. Jonas, can you please attach the expected spend vs. impact (delta TPS and p95 improvements) in the capacity_rollout_plan? I want to present a hard number to CFO.
- Pricing: Laura (that's me) -> I'll sync with Finance this afternoon. If we can't hold the margin floor, we should prioritize strategic customers (Acme) for limited discounting.
- QBR runbook: good start. Karthik, please confirm we have a named on-call and a post-mortem owner for the last two incidents.

Also — flagging that Legal will need to review the proposed short-term capacity addendum to the Dedicated T&Cs if we expect customers to rely on it. Can we get a 24–36h turnaround?

Laura

Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-06-19T08:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Weekly leadership signalcheck + QBR prep: decisions & handoffs

Adding the capacity plan and mitigation detail. Attachment: capacity_rollout_plan.pdf (attached).

Summary (short):
- Burst pool spec: 8x A10g-equivalent GPUs for 4 weeks (6/28–7/25). Estimated total incremental cost: $42k (net of committed discounts).
- Benefit: headroom +22% throughput at observed 90th percentile seq length, reduces queuing and maintains p95 under SLO during simulated holiday traffic.
- Rollout: canary on 6/26 to 10% traffic; full routing toggle on 6/28.
- Contingency: auto-fallback to hosted-small variant if any kernel regression spikes CPU.

On the model regression: research found a tokenizer edge-case in the reranker that affects a small customer cohort. We're pushing a fix today; canary will include reranker check runs. Owner: Models (Priya) + Runtime (Ethan).

I need Finance signoff for the $42k estimate to send to Procurement.

—Jonas

title: Post-demo: proposed evaluation cadence & gating criteria for Oriax Systems POC
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Patel <ava.patel@oriax.com>, Samuel Ortiz <sam.ortiz@oriax.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: proposed evaluation cadence & gating criteria for Oriax Systems POC

Hi Ava / Sam —

Thanks again for your time earlier. Quick recap and a concrete POC proposal attached (ORIAx_POC_Outline_v1.pdf) — aim is to keep the trial scoped and measurable so you can evaluate ROI in 3–4 weeks. Highlights below: 

Demo recap
- Walkthrough covered intent extraction, retrieval-augmented responses, and the redaction preview we discussed.
- Your team was focused on latency under 250ms for single-turn lookups and precision on entity extraction (F1 target >= 0.88).

Proposed POC: evaluation cadence & gating criteria (summary)
- Duration: 4 weeks active (plus 1 week buffer for setup).
- Success criteria (both must be met):
  * Latency: 95th percentile < 300ms for the primary endpoint under the test load (defined in the attached matrix).
  * Quality: Entity-extraction F1 >= 0.88 on the provided 2000-sample test set.
  * Stability: No more than 1 sustained error class in production-like tests during a 48h window.
- Cadence: weekly syncs (Mon, 30 min), mid-week check-ins as needed, daily monitoring dashboard access for your engineering team.
- Owners: Redwood delivery lead (Marissa Cole) for infra + default model config; Oriax leads: Ava (product) and Sam (engineering) — please confirm who will own data extract/ingest.
- Deliverables from Redwood: POC environment, test harness, monitoring dashboard, initial tuning pass, and a short final report.

Attachments: ORIAx_POC_Outline_v1.pdf (outline + timeline), oriax_requirements_matrix.xlsx (test definitions + sample mapping).

Next steps
1) Confirm owners for data ingest and security contact.
2) If agreed, kickoff meeting to schedule for week of Jun 21 — suggested: Tue Jun 22, 10:00am PT.
3) We'll provision a staging environment and request a short S3 transfer (or secure SFTP) for the sample dataset once kickoff is booked.

If anything in the attached needs tightening, tell me and we'll iterate. Happy to drop the timeline by a week if you want a leaner evaluation.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

--

References: HubSpot ORX-4621


From: Ava Patel <ava.patel@oriax.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Samuel Ortiz <sam.ortiz@oriax.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Jun 2027 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: proposed evaluation cadence & gating criteria for Oriax Systems POC

Ben —

Thanks for the clear recap and the attachments. A few quick clarifications before we lock dates: 

1) Data residency & security: our security team requires that any PII be redacted before transfer. Can Redwood provide an in-environment redaction step (we saw the preview) or do you expect us to pre-redact? Also please point me to the minimal SOC/Security artifacts to share with our InfoSec.
2) Sample volumes: the attached matrix references a 2k sample baseline — that's fine, but for load testing we should replicate a 10k short-queries mix. Can the harness scale to that?
3) Owners: I'll own product-level validation; Sam will own engineering & data movement. For security contact, CCing our InfoSec lead in follow-up (we'll share contact).

If all good, Sam and I are tentatively available Tue Jun 22 10am PT for kickoff.

Ava Patel
Head of Product, Oriax Systems
ava.patel@oriax.com | +1 (212) 555-2234

Ava / Sam —

Great — thanks for the quick reply and for assigning owners. Responses inline below and a couple of small updates to the plan: 

1) Data residency & security
- We recommend you do not pre-redact; Redwood can run an in-environment redaction pipeline before the data is materialized for model testing. That's what the redaction preview shows. We'll provide a short runbook and the SOC2 summary (attaching pointer) in a follow-up email to your InfoSec.

2) Sample volumes / load testing
- The harness can scale to the 10k short-query mix. For full parallelism and realistic 95th pctl latency we'll run load tests during week 3 in a dedicated capacity environment (no cross-tenant contention).

3) Kickoff & next steps
- Confirmed owners: Ava (product), Sam (engineering), Redwood delivery: Marissa Cole.
- Security contact: please share your InfoSec lead email and we'll include them on the data transfer plan.
- Kickoff meeting: Tue Jun 22, 10:00am PT — Ben/Marissa/Karthik + your Ava/Sam + Oriax InfoSec (optional). I'll send a calendar invite after you confirm InfoSec availability.

Minor adjustments to the POC outline:
- Added explicit security gate after week 1: if redaction or encryption requirements are incomplete, we pause stage testing until InfoSec sign-off (this is in the attached ORIAx_POC_Outline_v1.pdf).
- Added a short acceptance checklist for model outputs (owner: Ava) with two acceptance thresholds: soft (for exploratory tuning) and hard (final pass for success).

Attachments: ORIAx_POC_Outline_v1.pdf (updated) — unchanged file name, small security gate section added.

If that all looks good, I'll send the calendar invite and provisioning checklist today.

Thanks,
Ben
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

> - The harness can scale to the 10k short-query mix. For full parallelism and realistic 95th pctl latency we'll run load tests during week 3 in a dedicated capacity environment (no cross-tenant contention).
> 
> 3) Kickoff & next steps
> - Confirmed owners: Ava (product), Sam (engineering), Redwood delivery: Marissa Cole.
> - Security contact: please share your InfoSec lead email and we'll include them on the data transfer plan.
> - Kickoff meeting: Tue Jun 22, 10:00am PT — Ben/Marissa/Karthik + your Ava/Sam + Oriax InfoSec (optional). I'll send a calendar invite after you confirm InfoSec availability.
> 
> Minor adjustments to the POC outline:
> - Added explicit security gate after week 1: if redaction or encryption requirements are incomplete, we pause stage testing until InfoSec sign-off (this is in the attached ORIAx_POC_Outline_v1.pdf).
> - Added a short acceptance checklist for model outputs (owner: Ava) with two acceptance thresholds: soft (for exploratory tuning) and hard (final pass for success).
> 
> Attachments: ORIAx_POC_Outline_v1.pdf (updated) — unchanged file name, small security gate section added.
> 
> If that all looks good, I'll send the calendar invite and provisioning checklist today.
> 
> Thanks,
> Ben

title: Urgent: WebSocket stalls & p99 spikes during peak for Clearline — exec alerted
preview:
From: Claire Morris <claire.morris@clearline.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Paul Nguyen <paul.nguyen@clearline.ai>
Date: Thu, 17 Jun 2027 16:12:00 -0400
Subject: Re: user-impacting stalls on hosted websocket chat during 17:00-18:30 PT

Aditya — we started seeing a sharp increase in stalls and connection resets on your hosted websocket endpoint during our evening peak (17:00-18:30 PT). p95 is up ~2.1s (ok) but p99 is hitting ~21-28s for several requests and many clients show repeated reconnects. Attached a screenshot from our in-app telemetry and a small packet dump. This began ~17:05 and has persisted in ~40% of sessions.

This is impacting live customer interactions — some messages take so long they time out on our side. Can you confirm whether anything changed on your side or if this is a capacity/backpressure issue? We need an ETA on mitigation.

Attachments: screenshot_peak_1715.png, websocket_dumps_0617.tar.gz

Claire Morris
Head of Product Ops — Clearline Health
claire.morris@clearline.ai


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 17 Jun 2027 16:25:00 -0400
Subject: Fwd: user-impacting stalls on hosted websocket chat during 17:00-18:30 PT

Team — forwarding Clearline's report. They see p99s in the 20s and mass reconnects during their evening peak starting ~17:05 PT. They've attached a packet dump and screenshot (included). They haven't changed client side in the last 72h.

Please triage immediately. Ask: 1) can we identify a model / node-level hotspot? 2) can we enable an automatic fallback to a lower-latency model or force shorter-context responses as a quick mitigation? 3) are there any throttling/backpressure events in the router?

I'll keep the customer updated; Monica pls prep CS note.

-Aditya

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Claire Morris <claire.morris@clearline.ai>
Date: Thu, 17 Jun 2027 16:12:00 -0400
Subject: Re: user-impacting stalls on hosted websocket chat during 17:00-18:30 PT
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>

[original message and attachments]


From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 17 Jun 2027 16:48:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: user-impacting stalls on hosted websocket chat during 17:00-18:30 PT

Quick triage notes — I'm seeing the same p99 skew on our ingested traces in Datadog for the Clearline tenant. Initial observations:
- Around 17:03-17:07 PT there's a sharp uptick in long-tail token generation stalls tied to a subset of websocket connections that send bursty multi-chunk messages.
- Runtime traces show repeated KV cache misses and a rise in repeated model reloads on a small set of nodes (likely due to our eviction heuristics interacting poorly with their session patterns).
- No recent kernel or infra deploys in the region at that time; however, a config tweak to the eviction TTL rolled out yesterday may have increased cache churn under peak concurrency.

Immediate actions I'm proposing (can implement in next 30m):
1) Pin Clearline traffic to a warm pool (evict-safe) to avoid reloads — reduces cold starts but consumes reserved capacity.
2) Temporarily enable model-backoff/faster truncation for websocket sessions from this tenant to reduce per-request tokens (non-invasive fallback).
3) Increase router queue depth and apply stronger backpressure at the websocket ingress to prevent downstream overload.

Also requesting Clearline share a few sample session IDs (we have one from their dump) so we can reproduce locally.

I'll push a Datadog snapshot and a perf dump to the incident ticket.

-Dev


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 17 Jun 2027 17:10:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: user-impacting stalls on hosted websocket chat during 17:00-18:30 PT

I created an isolated warm pool and pinned Clearline for a failover test; also rolled back the TTL tweak on that pool. Early metrics (5m) show their p99 dropping from ~24s to ~3.7s for pinned traffic. Attached a short perf snapshot (dd_perf_snapshot.png).

I'm going to enable model-backoff (option 2 from Dev) for websocket sessions originating from their app key and toggle a more aggressive gating rule on the websocket ingress to shed load gracefully. These are non-disruptive to connections but will reduce token consumption.

If customer approves, we should schedule a 30m maintenance window to shift their production traffic to the pinned pool so we get stable behavior while we refine eviction heuristics.

-Evelyn

Attachment: dd_perf_snapshot.png


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Morris <claire.morris@clearline.ai>, Paul Nguyen <paul.nguyen@clearline.ai>, Clearline Ops <ops@clearline.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 17 Jun 2027 17:33:00 -04:00
Subject: RE: user-impacting stalls on hosted websocket chat during 17:00-18:30 PT

Claire — thanks for the quick report and for sending the dump. Engineering has identified cache churn caused by a recent TTL change as the likely trigger. We've spun up an isolated warm pool and enabled model-backoff for your websocket sessions; initial perf indicates p99 improvement.

Proposed next step: with your approval we'd like to perform a 30-minute maintenance window tomorrow at 17:00 PT to route your traffic to the pinned pool and observe behavior under peak. There will be no planned downtime — this is a controlled routing change to stabilize tail latency. We'll provide a post-window summary and escalate for SLA compensation if we confirm an impact on your side.

Monica from CS will coordinate customer-facing comms. Please confirm if tomorrow 17:00 PT works or propose alternatives.

-Aditya


From: Tom Baker <tom.baker@clearline.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Claire Morris <claire.morris@clearline.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 19 Jun 2027 17:45:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: user-impacting stalls on hosted websocket chat during 17:00-18:30 PT

Aditya / Monica — this is Tom (CTO). Our exec team is closely watching this — we had several large customers complain yesterday evening and our ops team recorded multiple session timeouts. We appreciate the mitigation so far but request an executive briefing (30m) to review root cause, customer impact, and a timeline for a durable fix to eviction heuristics.

Please propose two time slots Monday (06/21): 10:00 PT or 15:00 PT. We'd like Redwood engineering and product leadership on the call. Also include any interim mitigations and an estimate of potential SLA credit exposure.

Tom Baker
CTO — Clearline Health


(thread ends)

title: Pilot convergence & SDK adaptation plan — Novum Health discovery notes
preview:
From: Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>
To: Paula Lomax <paula@novumhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot convergence & SDK adaptation plan — Novum Health discovery notes

Thanks again for the call this morning, Paula — great conversation. I wanted to capture the key pain points, target goals, and an initial scoping approach so we have a single source of truth to share with your engineering and security teams.

Summary of what we heard (from Novum):
- Primary pain: triage routing in your intake UI is high-latency and brittle — clinicians see delays and misrouted cases, increasing manual workload.
- Secondary: downstream systems require structured outputs (discrete fields to populate EHR forms) rather than free text summaries.
- Ranking/retrieval: you’d like to combine embeddings-based retrieval with a lightweight reranker to improve candidate suggestions for diagnosis codes and referrals.
- Security/compliance: PHI in some flows; you need SOC 2 evidence, VPC/private deployment options, and tenant isolation for pilot data.

Proposed pilot scope (initial, 6-week loop):
1) Ingest: small, de-identified sample set (5k records) from Novum for evaluation; SE to help with schema.
2) Model path: prototype with open model + quantized variant for cost testing; evaluate embeddings + reranker for candidate generation.
3) Integration work: lightweight SDK adaptor to enable streaming responses in the triage UI and function-calling hooks to emit structured JSON field outputs.
4) Success metrics: latency p95 < 300ms for UI tokens, top-3 rerank accuracy up 15% vs baseline, end-to-end cost estimate within agreed threshold.

Immediate asks from our side to move forward:
- Can you share the engineering contact who owns the triage UI and an initial sample (de-identified) dataset?
- SOC 2 or security questionnaire contact (we’ll route our SOC 2 and architecture docs once NDA is in place).
- Preferred timeline for a short pilot kickoff (we proposed week of 6/28).

What we’ll deliver before kickoff:
- Draft pilot SOW and success criteria (attached: pilot-scope-draft.docx).
- One-page architecture diagram showing streaming + function-calling paths (attached: novum-arch-diagram-v1.pdf).
- A short list of the SDK changes we expect (streaming hooks, function call schema, structured-output serializers).

Next steps/meeting:
- If that aligns, I’ll send a 30-minute kickoff invite with our Solutions Engineer (Ben) and Marissa (Customer Success) copied here.

Thanks — happy to adjust scope based on your priorities.

Best,
Sergio Alvarez
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
sergio@redwood.com

---
Attachment stubs: novum-arch-diagram-v1.pdf (diagram, 1 page), pilot-scope-draft.docx (SOW draft)


From: Paula Lomax <paula@novumhealth.com>
To: Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>
Cc: Elliot Zhang <elliot.z@novumhealth.com>, Novum Health IT <it@novumhealth.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot convergence & SDK adaptation plan — Novum Health discovery notes

Thanks, Sergio — this is very helpful and aligned with what we discussed. A few clarifications and items from our side: 

- Dataset: we can provide a de-identified export (~4k records) by next Tue; Elliot will own the export and coordinate with your SE.
- Security: we do require SOC 2 Type II evidence and a short privacy questionnaire. Can you send that to it@novumhealth.com?
- Function calls / structured outputs: our EHR team will want the function schema in advance so they can map fields to the EHR form.
- Timeline: 6/28 kickoff works for us, but we’ll need the SOW and NDA in place first.

On the pilot goals: the latency targets look reasonable. One additional success criteria from our side — generated field population should avoid hallucinated codes; we’ll share a short list of constrained code sets to enforce.

I’m looping Elliot and IT on this thread; appreciate you including Ben and Marissa. Let us know if Redwood needs anything else for the NDA.

Paula
VP Product, Novum Health
paula@novumhealth.com

On Fri, Jun 18, 2027 at 09:12 AM Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks again for the call this morning, Paula — great conversation. I wanted to capture the key pain points...


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 13:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Novum pilot — SE/engineering asks

Quick internal alignment following the discovery write-up. Actions I’m taking / requesting: 
- SE assignment: I’ll own initial SE bandwidth and will slot Chris (solutions_eng) for kickoff. Chris should scope the SDK changes: streaming integration (client-side token streaming), function-calling schema, and a serializer to emit structured JSON fields for the EHR mapping.
- Data: we need a sample CSV and a simple mapping doc from Novum (Elliot) before we start. Sergio — please confirm we requested that.
- Legal/compliance: ask Legal to prep a standard NDA and route SOC 2 docs to their IT contact. Marissa — can you follow up with Legal and attach our SOC 2 pack to the HubSpot deal?
- Prioritization matrix (rough):
  * High: Structured output schema, PHI handling / isolation (must-have)
  * Medium: Streaming for UI responsiveness (important for clinician UX)
  * Low: Advanced reranker tuning (nice-to-have for pilot; can be iterative)

I’ll draft the technical kickoff agenda and send a proposed invite after we have NDA. Keen to keep this lightweight for 6-week cycle.

-Ben
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com


From: Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>
To: Paula Lomax <paula@novumhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Elliot Zhang <elliot.z@novumhealth.com>, Novum Health IT <it@novumhealth.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Jun 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot convergence & SDK adaptation plan — Novum Health discovery notes

Paula — thanks for the quick confirmation. We’ll proceed as follows: 

1) Legal/NDA: Marissa will route our standard NDA and attach SOC 2 artifacts to HubSpot (deal NH-2027-0821). Expect an email from legal@redwood.com by EOD Mon.
2) Data handoff: Elliot — when you have the de-id sample, please upload to the Drive link in my earlier email and send a short mapping excel. Our SE (Chris) will use that to produce the function call schema.
3) Kickoff: tentatively 6/28 at 10:00am PT — 30 minutes. Proposed attendees: Paula, Elliot, Novum IT, Sergio, Ben (SE), Marissa (CS). I’ll send a calendar invite after NDA is in place.
4) Deliverables week 1: function-call schema draft + SDK adaptor change list; week 2: streaming PoC in staging; week 4–6: rerank tuning + evaluation.

Attached: novum-arch-diagram-v1.pdf (architecture sketch).

If that works, I’ll mark the HubSpot deal and attach the draft SOW; Ben will pick up SE tasks once Chris is scheduled.

Thanks — looking forward to moving quickly.

Sergio Alvarez
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
sergio@redwood.com

title: Reserved capacity proposal: OrbitAI GPU slab allocation & cost-sharing structure
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: OrbitAI - proposal for prorated GPU slab + cost-sharing structure

Team —

Following the demo last week, OrbitAI (Olivia & procurement) is asking for a reserved capacity structure that gives them predictability for peak inference while reducing near-term cash outlay. They want a 24–36 month commit but are sensitive to spot GPU-market volatility. Key asks from Orbit:

- A baseline reserved slab (dedicated vGPU pools) sized for 60% of forecasted peak Q4 load with an option to burst to hosted capacity.
- A price-floor / corridor when GPU spot cost moves >15% vs baseline (they asked for a pass-through cap).
- Phased activation (prorated billing) during first 6 months to align with product ramp.

I attached the capacity model and a suggested SKU matrix with three scenarios (Conservative / Base / Aggressive). I proposed a blended discount band that increases with committed GPU hours and a cost-sharing clause for >20% GPU-market delta.

Asking for: 1) a quick pricing sensitivity from finance/PMM so we can respond within 48h, and 2) guidance on whether we can offer a prorated activation without breaking existing discount bands.

Attachment: [Attachment: orbitai_capacity_model.xlsx (xlsx, 48KB)]
Attachment: [Attachment: proposed_sku_matrix.pdf (pdf, 180KB)]

—Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 16 Jun 2027 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: OrbitAI - proposal for prorated GPU slab + cost-sharing structure

Ben —

Thanks for the context and the model. Quick thoughts from pricing sensitivity perspective:

- The three-scenario model is helpful. If we anchor the \"Base\" slab at an ASA (average sustained allocation) that maps to ~200 A100-equivalent hours/day, the blended discount to customer at 12-months starts at ~24% off list and can tier to 34% at the 36-month / top-slab.
- For GPU cost pass-throughs: finance is generally uncomfortable with unconditional variable pass-throughs. We can offer a capped corridor: if Redwood's average GPU procurement cost (monthly rolling) increases >18% vs baseline, we trigger either (a) a one-time credit to the customer up to X% of the monthly invoice or (b) an automatic discount step for the remainder of the quarter. Option (b) preserves invoice clarity and minimizes accounting churn.
- Prorated activation: operationally feasible — we can structure month-1 and month-2 at 50% billable on committed slab while counting full allocation for capacity planning. That preserves ramp incentives while giving OrbitAI an OPEX-friendly entry.

I sketched three pricing levers in the attached \"pricing_notes\" link (see Drive). If Orbit wants a deeper hedge, we can propose a \"price-rebalance\" clause that reindexes discount tiers to an agreed GPU-price index every 12 months. That would require legal and finance signoff.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Deal Economics, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai

On Wed, Jun 16, 2027 at 07:55 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks — attaching the scenario workbook now for reference.


From: Olivia Park <olivia@orbitai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Fisher <jordan@orbitai.com>, procurement@orbitai.com
Date: Thu, 17 Jun 2027 11:30:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Reserved capacity proposal: OrbitAI GPU slab allocation & cost-sharing structure

Ben —

Appreciate the quick turnaround and the scenarios. A few clarifying points from Orbit's side before we take this to our CFO/procurement:

1) We need predictable unit economics for our customers; a corridor-trigger model is acceptable if the customer-facing price impact is limited and predictable. Can you confirm the maximum monthly credit % you're willing to cap at under option (a)?

2) Regarding prorated activation: we like the 50% billing for the first two months but need prorated ramp applied by actual activation date (not the contract start). Can you confirm this is operationally supported?

3) SLA & capacity guarantee: for the reserved slab, can you confirm a 99.5% availability SLA at the node level and a defined replacement timeline for failed GPUs (we need specifics for our SOC review).

4) Finally — discounts. Our procurement prefers a 30% floor on the blended discount for a 24-month commit with a quarterly reprice floor if market prices spike. Is pushing to a 30% blended floor possible for Orbit if we accept the 24-month tenor and a 20% prepayment?

We can be available for a quick call Friday.

Best,
Olivia
Head of Infrastructure, OrbitAI
olivia@orbitai.com | +1 650-555-0124


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Olivia Park <olivia@orbitai.com>, procurement@orbitai.com
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Reserved capacity proposal: OrbitAI GPU slab allocation & cost-sharing structure

Olivia / Ben / team —

Thanks — looping in Laura from Legal and Finance for quick approvals. Short answers inline:

1) Maximum monthly credit cap: Finance is comfortable with a cap at 12% of the monthly committed invoice under the credit route (option (a)). Alternatively, we can implement option (b) — a quarter-level discount step that scales with the delta. We recommend option (b) operationally.

2) Prorated activation by actual activation date: operationally supported. We'll include an activation window clause: customers have a 90-day activation window from contract signing; billing is pro-rated to activation date within that window. After 90 days, the full committed slab invoicing begins.

3) SLA & replacement timeline: we can commit to 99.5% availability at the node level for Dedicated pools. Replacement SLA: hot-spare substitution within 6 hours for critical nodes, full hardware replacement/in-service remediation within 72 hours. We'll provide the detailed SLA addendum in the next doc iteration.

4) Discount floor: We can offer a 30% blended discount floor for a 24-month commitment if Orbit agrees to a 20% prepayment and accepts a 12% capped monthly credit (or the quarterly discount step). This requires EMEA/Finance exec approval for the blended floor at our top-slab; I've flagged it for expedited review.

Next steps: Ben, please confirm that Orbit is comfortable with the 20% prepay + 90-day activation window and whether they prefer the credit (immediate but capped) vs. the quarterly discount step (simpler invoicing). I'll draft an addendum for the SLA + activation language once you confirm.

—Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Head of Revenue Operations, Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwood.ai

title: Triforce dedicated capacity: tri-region sizing + onramp playbook
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Ava Thomas <ava@triforce.ai>, HelixOps <ops@helixops.com>
Cc: procurement@triforce.ai
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Triforce tri-region dedicated ask — initial context and ask

Team — looping you in on Triforce's request for a tri-region (US / EU / APAC) dedicated footprint tied to their Q4 deploy. Quick context: Triforce is an autonomy startup that will be moving from batch-only inference to a low-latency endpoint for their agent orchestration layer. They flagged predictable token volume and bursty daytime traffic across NA/EU/APAC.

Ava / procurement — thanks for the early heads-up. High-level asks from the customer:
- Guaranteed baseline capacity per region (sustained throughput) + burst pool for traffic spikes
- Option to convert burst to reserved if utilization holds after 3 months
- Prefer Redwood Dedicated but open to hybrid (Dedicated + Hosted fallback)

Internal asks: can we put together a first-pass sizing and an onramp playbook (capacity + timing + SLOs + estimated monthly committed cost) they can take to procurement? I attached the sizing workbook + a short onramp playbook draft.

Marissa — can you run a capacity estimate given the workload notes below? Karthik / Irene — want to flag any known region supply constraints we should surface.

Workload notes (from Triforce):
- Avg token/sec: US 7k, EU 3k, APAC 2k (with 3x daytime spikes in US and 2x in APAC)
- Latency target: p95 < 120ms across regions
- Model: mix of 13B and 70B variants; 70B invoked for heavy reranking/agent planning, ~25% of requests
- Timeline: pilot in-stage (staging) 2027-08, production ramp Q4 (target commit by 2027-10)

Thanks — attaching workbook.

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0147

Attachment: triforce_sizing_workbook.xlsx (spreadsheet, 120KB)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Ava Thomas <ava@triforce.ai>
Cc: HelixOps <ops@helixops.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 11:38:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Triforce tri-region dedicated ask — initial context and ask

Thanks Ben — reviewed the workload notes and workbook. High-level preliminary sizing (very conservative):
- US: baseline pool ~8 x A100-80GB-equivalent nodes (to cover sustained 7k token/sec, mixed models), burst pool +4 nodes for daytime spikes
- EU: baseline pool ~4 x A100-80GB-equivalent nodes, burst +2
- APAC: baseline pool ~3 x A100-80GB-equivalent nodes, burst +2 (Singapore edge preferred)

Sizing assumptions: 13B ~1.2k tokens/sec per GPU, 70B ~400 tokens/sec per GPU (quantized runtime). Batching and KV cache expected to improve effective throughput ~20% over raw.

Questions / risks:
1) Model mix volatility — if 70B usage rises above 30% we need to re-run sizing.
2) Onboarding timeline — US capacity is available in our committed pools but EU/APAC will require a short lead (~6–8 weeks) to guarantee reserved nodes for Q4 without interfering with existing tranche commitments.
3) Network egress patterns — if they want cross-region redundancy we'll need to map failover lanes.

Irene / Karthik — please confirm supply lead times for EU and SG. I'll draft an onramp playbook (steps, gating metrics, canary plan) and share a short SOW template.

—
Marissa Cole
Head of Dedicated Programs, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

Attachment: initial_onramp_playbook.pdf (PDF, 48KB)

From: Ava Thomas <ava@triforce.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@triforce.ai, HelixOps <ops@helixops.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Jun 2027 02:05:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Triforce tri-region dedicated ask — initial context and ask

Marissa / Ben — thanks, this is really helpful. A couple clarifications from our side:
- The daytime spikes in US are predictable (8am–10am PT); APAC spikes align with APAC business hours but are shorter. We can provide historical token profiles for the last 6 weeks.
- We intend to keep the 70B traffic at ~20–25% initially but expect it may rise with new features — we want a path to scale without a long reprovisioning lead time.
- Procurement asked whether a soft-commit (3-month trial with conversion option) is feasible. They also want an SLA for burst allocation (time to spin up burst nodes and guaranteed priority) — is that an add-on?

HelixOps — can you confirm whether Singapore nodes have any procurement blackout windows in Q3? If so we need to plan alternate APAC placement.

Ava Thomas
Director, Platform @ Triforce.ai
ava@triforce.ai

> On Tue, Jun 15, 2027 at 11:38 Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Sizing assumptions: 13B ~1.2k tokens/sec per GPU, 70B ~400 tokens/sec per GPU (quantized runtime).
> > ...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Thomas <ava@triforce.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, HelixOps <ops@helixops.com>
Cc: procurement@triforce.ai
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 17:43:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Triforce tri-region dedicated ask — initial context and ask

Ava / team — thanks for the quick feedback. Action plan and asks below (short MRAP):

1) Internal: Marissa to finalize a capacity plan and a cost estimate based on the conservative sizing above, plus a second column showing a 30% 70B usage sensitivity. Deliverable: updated workbook by 2027-06-21 EOD.
2) Internal: Karthik / Irene to confirm EU and SG supply lead-times and whether we can offer a 3-month trial with conversion option without blocking other tranche commitments. Deliverable: status update 2027-06-20.
3) External: Triforce to share 6-week token profile and preferred failover lanes for cross-region redundancy. Deliverable: Triforce to upload to the shared link by 2027-06-20.
4) Commercial: I will circulate a one-page trial conversion term sheet (soft-commit -> reserved conversion option) and an add-on for \"burst SLA\" (priority scheduling + <15min node allocation window for approved bursts).

Notes on cost framing: baseline committed pools will be priced as reserved capacity; burst SLA will carry a premium and/or a short-term billing uplift when invoked. We'll call out a conversion credit if they convert trial to reserved within 90 days.

Next meeting — propose a 30-min runbook review on Monday 2027-06-21 10:00 PT. Ben, can you confirm? Ava / procurement — does that slot work?

Cheers,
Ben

Onwards: quoting the latest sizing doc and playbook attached.

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0147

title: Proposal: Stepwise Commit Rebalance & Temporary Price Guard
preview:
From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@arborcloud.io>, Elena Ruiz <elena@arborcloud.io>
Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-14T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Proposal: Stepwise Commit Rebalance & Temporary Price Guard

Hi Samir / Elena,

Thanks again for the time last week — great to see the ArborCloud product team lined up for the pilot extension. Per our renewal window (contract ends 2027-07-31), we put together a short, pragmatic proposal that balances your need for predictable pricing during the Q3 migration and our capacity planning for the coming quarter. Attached is a one-page term summary and a draft term sheet (AV-ARBR-term-rebalance-v1.pdf).

High level (we can iterate):
- Term: 12 months starting 2027-08-01 with staged commit increases (see table in attachment): 0–90 days: current commit baseline; 91–180 days: +30% commit; 181–365 days: +60% commit.
- Temporary Price Guard: a 90-day fixed effective rate for the baseline model families used in your workload (applies to first tranche). This protects you from short-term market-driven price movement while we validate throughput during the migration.
- One-time onboarding credit: $25k credit applied as invoice offset if volume-based onboarding milestones hit in the first 60 days.
- True-up & monthly invoicing: monthly invoices with a quarterly true-up to reconcile usage against commit.

Questions for you:
1) Would ArborCloud accept staged commit triggers (activity-based) or prefer calendar-based tranches?
2) Procurement timing — do you need an executed PO before 2027-07-15 to meet your internal renewal window?

If this looks directionally right I can run the numbers with David (pricing) and Priya (CS/ops) and send a redline-ready copy for your legal/procurement. Happy to set a 30-min call tomorrow afternoon.

Thanks,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: AV-ARBR-term-rebalance-v1.pdf (term summary, draft schedule)


From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@arborcloud.io>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Elena Ruiz <elena@arborcloud.io>
Date: 2027-06-15T11:07:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: Stepwise Commit Rebalance & Temporary Price Guard

- Trigger mechanics: activity-based triggers sound fair, but alerts and a simple dashboard view will be required so our SREs can track when tranches move. Can you include monitoring SLAs in the term sheet?
- Billing proration: if we sign 08/01 and actual usage ramps slower, will the first tranche be prorated or invoiced at commit with a subsequent credit?

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-16T09:32:00-07:00

Avery / Priya — looping on pricing mechanics. Quick notes after reviewing Samir's asks (quoted below).

> - Price guard scope: is the fixed rate limited to the baseline family only (we mainly use rwd-base and rwd-2b), or does it cover any model we run within the first 90 days?
> - Trigger mechanics: activity-based triggers sound fair...
> - Billing proration: if we sign 08/01 and actual usage ramps slower, will the first tranche be prorated or invoiced at commit with a subsequent credit?

Proposal from pricing side (draft; can soften):
- Price Guard: lock applies to explicitly listed model families: rwd-base and rwd-2b only (we can add rwd-lite as an exception). We will list families in the term sheet.
- Tranche pricing: 4% blended discount on the first tranche above baseline (applies during 0–90 days effective rate), 2% blended discount on subsequent tranches. Discounts are applied to per-token charges, not on fixed reserved fees.
- Proration: invoice at commit (no retro proration). If usage is below commit at the quarter true-up, we will apply a one-time credit up to 50% of the onboarding credit (so net downside protection) — this protects you while keeping clear economic signals.

If you want, I can draft exact table numbers (monthly commit, per-token rate, discount band) and a fallback clause for model substitution. Also recommend we add a simple dashboard requirement for tranche visibility (ops deliverable from our CS team).

—David
David Kim
Director, Pricing | Redwood Inference

- Discounts: 4% blended discount applied to per-token rates for the first tranche (baseline->+30%), 2% blended for subsequent tranches. Discounts do not apply to fixed reserved capacity fees.

Hi Maya / StellarAV team —

Thanks again for the quick turn. We’re finalizing the tech plan for the Redwood x Acme enterprise workshop on July 20–21 (Main Hall + two breakout rooms). High level asks:

- Primary: full-duplex hybrid room for Main Hall (100 pax, U-shaped seating) with a dedicated conference hub for remote execs (4 remote seats).

4) Network: we recommend a dedicated event VLAN (we’ll bring our own router for NAT and QoS). If venue can guarantee 150 Mbps up on VLAN, we’ll use that; otherwise we’ll bring a bonded LTE fallback (adds ~10–15s failover time).

Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity proposal + SKU mapping (Skyline)

Camila —

Thanks for the doc. A couple of procurement red flags from our side before we move to legal:

1) Unit definition: The Sheet defines PU as A100-equivalent vGPU-hours, but our infra team needs to see a clearer definition for multi-architecture support (we run a mix of A100 / H100 epochs). Can you add an explicit equivalence table?

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-14T15:05:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Dedicated capacity proposal + procurement questions (Skyline)

Marissa / Ben —

Forwarding Skyline's procurement ask. They want: clearer PU equivalence across hardware, a tiered remedy ladder, and two example invoices showing burst behavior. Quick thoughts below and I've attached our internal SKU mapping and a 1-page redline summary.

Marissa: can you draft a remedy ladder that ties credits to the measured PU shortfall over a 30-day window? Ideally keep maximum monthly credit capped so we don't exceed 50% of the committed monthly fee. Suggestion:
- Shortfall 0-10%: 5% credit
- Shortfall 10-25%: 15% credit
- Shortfall 25-50%: 35% credit
- Shortfall >50%: 50% credit + escalation

Also propose defining "Committed GPU Pool Unit (CGPU-U)" so legal has a canonical term (1 CGPU-U = 1 reserved vGPU-equivalent, measured hourly; vendor provides equivalence table for H100/A100 variations).

Ben: will Redwood accept a measurement window of 30 days and a monthly cap at 50%? Legal should redline language around measurement (METRICS: 95th-percentile sustained throughput vs instantaneous peaks) and whether burst buffer consumption is billable.

Attachments: sku_mapping_for_legal.xlsx, redline_summary.docx

--
Camila


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Natalie Rivera <natalie.rivera@skylinebio.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-16T09:20:00-07:00
Subject: Revised term sheet & remedy ladder (Skyline)

Hi Natalie —

Thanks for the clear ask. We've incorporated the changes and attached a redlined term sheet. Summary of substantive updates (see attached term_sheet_redline_20270616.pdf):

- Unit definitions: Introduced canonical term "Committed GPU Pool Unit (CGPU-U)". 1 CGPU-U = 1 A100-equivalent reserved vGPU-hour. Appendix A contains an equivalence table (1 H100 = 1.6 A100-equivalent CGPU-U; smaller SKU ratios listed).

- Remedy ladder: We adopted Marissa's ladder with one change: cap the maximum single-month credit at 50% of the committed monthly fee and limit cumulative credits to 100% over any 12-month rolling period. Measurement window = calendar month; metric = % of hours where effective sustained throughput fell below 95% of the committed profile for that PU band. Credits prorated by hours impacted.

- Burst buffer: We added a 72-hour monthly burst buffer pool that is billable only when consumed beyond 10% of monthly committed PU hours; consumption beyond that converts to on-demand at our burst rate. Examples & two sample invoice pages included in Appendix B.

- Pricing: 12-month commit discounts remain as previously proposed; we've added a one-time onboarding credit line item (can be used as a one-time invoice credit).

Next steps: If this looks good, we'd issue a DocuSign term sheet and accept a PO referencing the deal id. I've also included a short HubSpot link below for the record.

Attachments: term_sheet_redline_20270616.pdf
Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/12345/skyline-bio

Thanks,
Camila

To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
