title: Residency guarantees and deletion evidence for Private VPC — audit request
preview:
From: Katie Smythe <katie.smythe@meditex.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: security@meditex.com
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Residency guarantees & deletion evidence for our Private VPC

Hi Ava,

We're preparing for an internal compliance review at Meditex next quarter and need to collect evidence that our Private VPC deployment with Redwood meets our residency and deletion guarantees. Specifically, we need: 
- A short attestation that customer data for our tenant is stored only in the specified US region and will not be replicated outside that region without explicit approval.
- A description of the deletion workflow and expected timelines (what happens when we request a tenant erase, and how long before backups/snapshots are removed).
- Any available logs or audit artifacts (operations/certifications) you can provide or point us to that would satisfy our auditors.

Can you provide an initial packet or point us to where we can access this? Also indicate any evidence that requires an NDA or formal attestation/Docusign.

Thanks,
Katie
Katie Smythe
Head of IT Compliance
Meditex Health
---
Confidentiality note: internal regulatory request

To: Katie Smythe <katie.smythe@meditex.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Residency guarantees & deletion evidence for our Private VPC

Hi Katie — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Marissa (account lead) and Vivek (infra/ops) so we can assemble the packet. Quick clarifying Qs to make sure we give the right artifacts:
1) When you say \"specified US region\" — is Meditex requiring a single region (e.g., us-west-2) for all tenant data, or is multi-AZ within the same region acceptable?
2) Do auditors require cryptographic proof of key ownership (customer-managed KMS) or is attestations + access logs sufficient?

Planned deliverables from our side:
- Redwood attestation PDF confirming region binding and non-replication policy for the tenant.
- Overview of the erase workflow (what gets removed, typical timelines, how snapshots/backups are handled).
- Sample audit artifacts: retention logs, erase request ticket example, and relevant Console screenshots (redacted).
- Links to SOC 2 artefacts and any redwood public docs relevant to Private deployments.

If you need a signed attestation we can route a short DocuSign. Vivek — can you prepare the attestation and a diagram of the erase workflow? Marissa — I will coordinate delivery and any procurement/NDA paperwork.

Best,
Ava
Ava Chen
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Katie Smythe <katie.smythe@meditex.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 11:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Residency guarantees & deletion evidence for our Private VPC

Katie — following up with the plan and timeline. We can deliver an initial evidence packet in 48 business hours. Items and notes below: 

1) Region binding attestation (deliverable: PDF) — confirms: tenant data physically stored in your chosen region (single-region binding: yes, multi-region replication only with explicit customer consent).
2) Erase workflow doc (deliverable: diagram + short narrative) — explains steps when Meditex requests a tenant wipe:
   - Customer issues erase via Console or support ticket (timestamped)
   - System marks tenant for teardown; running jobs are drained, new requests are rejected
   - KV- and prefix-caches are invalidated; underlying storage objects are scheduled for deletion
   - Snapshots/backups: policy-controlled. By default, backups are retained for 30 days unless set otherwise; we can provide a 0-day retention policy for the tenant as part of the agreement.
   - Final purge: object deletion + metadata scrub, and a verification job that runs up to 7 days post-request.
3) Audit artifacts — we will include: example erase ticket exports (redacted), storage delete event log snippets (ISO timestamps), and Console trace for erase operation.
4) KMS/key ownership — Meditex can use customer-managed KMS; logs of KMS usage and key policies are included where relevant. If you require a cryptographic attestation (key wrapping proof), that will require a short ops playbook and possibly signature via DocuSign.

Attachments we plan to send: residency-attestation-2027-06.pdf, vpc-erase-workflow-diagram.png. If the auditors need a signed legal attestation, note that in reply and we'll generate a DocuSign packet.

Marissa

Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference

Date: Wed, 16 Jun 2027 08:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Residency attestation & erase workflow (packet attached)

Hi Katie — attaching the initial packet here:
- residency-attestation-2027-06.pdf (formal attestation: single-region binding, replication policy, retention default settings)
- vpc-erase-workflow-diagram.png (technical flow showing staging, delete, verification windows)

Attachment stubs: [Attachment: residency-attestation-2027-06.pdf (application/pdf, 84KB)] [Attachment: vpc-erase-workflow-diagram.png (image/png, 48KB)]

Technical notes (high level):
- No automatic cross-region replication for Private VPC tenants unless configured at onboarding; cross-region DR requires explicit customer opt-in and written approval.
- Erase timeline: object deletion requests are executed within minutes; dependent snapshots/backups follow retention policy and are queued for purge; final verification completes within 7 days unless you request accelerated purge.
- For customers using customer-managed KMS, Redwood does not hold the plaintext key; keys remain under customer KMS control and we log KMS decrypt operations in the audit trail.

If you want the attestation signed by our legal team, I'll initiate a DocuSign and include the legal sign-off. Otherwise this packet should be sufficient for technical auditors.

title: Kickoff: security questionnaire + options for logs / deletion SLA
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@medsys.com>, Sara Li <sara.li@medsys.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Kickoff: security questionnaire + options for logs / deletion SLA

Hi Liam / Sara — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and next steps so we have alignment:  

- SOC 2 status: Redwood has a current SOC 2 Type II report (controls through Mar 2027). We'll share a redacted summary and a process note on how we handle evidence requests.  
- Questionnaire: attached is our standard security questionnaire (xlsx). Please start with the sections on data residency, encryption, and incident response; your infosec team flagged log retention + backups as priority items.  
- Retention & deletion ask: we heard you want clarity on (a) log retention windows, (b) deletion guarantees for data-in-use and backups, and (c) how deletion is handled across snapshots/backups. We propose documenting three options (below) for your legal/risk team to pick from.  

Proposed retention/deletion options (for discussion)
- Option A (Default, lowest latency): Application logs 30 days; backup snapshots retained 90 days; deletion pipeline tombstones immediately, full purge within 30 days; backups encrypted with customer-managed keys optional.
- Option B (Audit-oriented): Application logs 90 days; backups retained 180 days; purge window up to 90 days for historical archives; additional WORM support where required.
- Option C (Regulated): Custom per-contract: e.g., HIPAA workflows with 365+ retention, tailored deletion certificates and audit exports.

Next steps / timeline
1) Please complete the attached questionnaire (highlight sections) and return by EOD Wed 6/16 if possible.  
2) We will upload a redacted SOC 2 summary and a short Deletion & Backup SLA options doc to the shared Drive (link below) by EOD 6/14.  
3) If you want, we can schedule a 30-min follow-up to walk through the questionnaire and the options — suggest Thu 6/17 or Fri 6/18.  

Attachments: Redwood_Security_Questionnaire_v1.xlsx (questionnaire), Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_Summary.pdf (summary), Deletion_and_Backup_SLA_options.docx (options).

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0142

---
Note: internal recipients only — Monica / Aditya, please see the bolded sections in the questionnaire where MedSys typically asks for expanded notes on backups + encryption.

From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@medsys.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sara Li <sara.li@medsys.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 10:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Kickoff: security questionnaire + options for logs / deletion SLA

Thanks Ben — good summary. A couple clarifications from our security team after a quick pass:  

- Backup retention: our default procurement baseline requires backups to be retained a minimum of 90 days, with the ability to request up to 365 days for certain PHI-related datasets. We need confirmation on how deletion requests are propagated to backups/snapshots — specifically: are backups ever re-encrypted or rewritten as part of deletion, or is deletion handled via metadata tombstones until snapshot expiry?
- Deletion SLA: our security policy expects a vendor to offer a documented deletion process and evidence (audit log) that deletion completed. We would consider a 30-day purge window acceptable if accompanied by a deletion certificate or audit entry.
- Encryption & keys: please confirm whether Redwood supports customer-managed keys (CMK) for both primary data and backups, and whether key rotation affects retained backups.

We can aim to return the questionnaire by end-of-day Friday 6/18. Would prefer a short call to walk through the options — Thu 6/17 at 11:00am ET works for Sara + me.  

Regards,
Liam O'Connor
Head of InfoSec | MedSys Health
liam.oconnor@medsys.com

> ...
> Attachments: Redwood_Security_Questionnaire_v1.xlsx (questionnaire), Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_Summary.pdf (summary), Deletion_and_Backup_SLA_options.docx (options).
> 
> Thanks — Ben


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 13:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: answers + recommended language for MedSys questionnaire

Ben / Aditya — internal notes to shape our response and the SLA language we should offer:  

- Backups: Redwood treats backups as immutable snapshots for retention windows; logical deletion marks records as deleted immediately and removes them from active serving within seconds, but snapshots include the records until snapshot retention expiry. We can offer a "certified purge" option where we rebuild snapshot indexes to exclude deleted records and produce an audit certificate, for an additional compliance fee.
- Default offering to propose: application/log retention 30 days + backups 90 days (Option A) with a documented upgrade path to 180/365 for regulated accounts. This aligns to our standard infra and avoids operationally expensive snapshot rewrites for most customers.
- CMK: we support CMK via KMS integrations for both at-rest and snapshot encryption. Rotation is supported; rotated keys remain usable for decrypting older snapshots unless a re-encryption is explicitly requested (that is an ops task and typically tied to a contract).
- Evidence: we can provide an audit export showing deletion events and snapshot expiry times; we generate a deletion certificate on request (SLA-based deliverable).

Suggested wording to send to MedSys (short):
"Redwood's standard model: logical deletion happens immediately; full purge from backups occurs at snapshot expiry (default 90 days). For customers requiring a certified purge before snapshot expiry, Redwood offers a documented certified-purge process (rebuild & audit) as an add-on. We also support CMK for both live data and backups; key rotation does not automatically re-encrypt snapshots unless requested."

I also attached a redlined Deletion_and_Backup_SLA_options.docx for Ben to send.

Monica
Monica Patel
Security Solutions | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.ai

> On Tue, Jun 15, 2027 at 10:03 AM Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@medsys.com> wrote:
> > Thanks Ben — good summary. A couple clarifications from our security team after a quick pass:  
> > ...

1) Confirmations based on internal review (see Monica's notes):
- Default behavior: logical deletion is immediate; records removed from active indexes within seconds. Snapshots/backups retain data until the configured snapshot expiry. Default snapshot expiry we propose for MedSys is 90 days.
- Certified purge: available as an add-on; rebuilds snapshot indexes and provides an audit certificate proving removal prior to snapshot expiry. Typical lead time: 5–10 business days depending on dataset size.
- CMK: supported for primary and backups. Key rotation is supported; re-encryption of historical snapshots is a separate, billable operation.
- Deletion evidence: we can provide an audit export and a signed deletion certificate upon request (in SLA).

- Questionnaire: attached is our standard security questionnaire (xlsx). Please start with the sections on data residency, encryption, and incident response; your infosec team flagged log retention + backups as priority items.  
- Retention & deletion ask: we heard you want clarity on (a) log retention windows, (b) deletion guarantees for data-in-use and backups, and (c) how deletion is handled across snapshots/backups. We propose documenting three options (below) for your legal/risk team to pick from.  

Proposed retention/deletion options (for discussion)
- Option A (Default, lowest latency): Application logs 30 days; backup snapshots retained 90 days; deletion pipeline tombstones immediately, full purge within 30 days; backups encrypted with customer-managed keys optional.
- Option B (Audit-oriented): Application logs 90 days; backups retained 180 days; purge window up to 90 days for historical archives; additional WORM support where required.
- Option C (Regulated): Custom per-contract: e.g., HIPAA workflows with 365+ retention, tailored deletion certificates and audit exports.

Next steps / timeline

Ben / Aditya — internal notes to shape our response and the SLA language we should offer:  

- Backups: Redwood treats backups as immutable snapshots for retention windows; logical deletion marks records as deleted immediately and removes them from active serving within seconds, but snapshots include the records until snapshot retention expiry. We can offer a "certified purge" option where we rebuild snapshot indexes to exclude deleted records and produce an audit certificate, for an additional compliance fee.
- Default offering to propose: application/log retention 30 days + backups 90 days (Option A) with a documented upgrade path to 180/365 for regulated accounts. This aligns to our standard infra and avoids operationally expensive snapshot rewrites for most customers.
- CMK: we support CMK via KMS integrations for both at-rest and snapshot encryption. Rotation is supported; rotated keys remain usable for decrypting older snapshots unless a re-encryption is explicitly requested (that is an ops task and typically tied to a contract).
- Evidence: we can provide an audit export showing deletion events and snapshot expiry times; we generate a deletion certificate on request (SLA-based deliverable).

Suggested wording to send to MedSys (short):
"Redwood's standard model: logical deletion happens immediately; full purge from backups occurs at snapshot expiry (default 90 days). For customers requiring a certified purge before snapshot expiry, Redwood offers a documented certified-purge process (rebuild & audit) as an add-on. We also support CMK for both live data and backups; key rotation does not automatically re-encrypt snapshots unless requested."

I also attached a redlined Deletion_and_Backup_SLA_options.docx for Ben to send.

Monica

title: Suggested inference engines & prioritized shortlist — Summit demo follow-up
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@summit.dev>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Summit Tech Ops <engops@summit.dev>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Suggested inference engines & prioritized shortlist — Summit demo follow-up

Hi Samira — thanks again for the demo time today. Quick recap and a recommended shortlist of engines for your two main use-cases (chat assistance + batch document summarization). I kept the notes focused on cost/latency/quality tradeoffs and next steps so your engineering team can pick a path for an early evaluation.

Executive summary
- Recommendation: run a two-track eval in parallel for 2 weeks: a low-cost fast-path and a high-quality fallback. This balances throughput for interactive assist with quality for multi-page summarization.
- Shortlist (priority order): 1) redwood-rw-medium-quant (cost-efficient, strong latency for chat), 2) open-xl-13B-8bit (balanced quality/cost), 3) redwood-rw-xl-hq (highest quality, higher cost — use as fallback).

Why these three?
- redwood-rw-medium-quant: ~1.8x cost improvement vs unfused 8-bit XL on our benchmarks for short chats (<=512 tokens) with negligible quality regression in instruction-following. Median P95 latency ~110ms on our dedicated instance. Ideal to serve interactive chat at scale.
- open-xl-13B-8bit: more consistent on longer contexts (1k-2k tokens) with modest latency increase (~220ms P95) and noticeably better summarization coherence than the medium quant variant. Good middle ground if you want a single engine to cover both flows.
- redwood-rw-xl-hq: best-in-class for multi-page summarization and tricky context disambiguation. Cost is ~3-4x the medium quant option per token; use as a canary/fallback for expensive jobs or critical customer flows.

Benchmarks and artifacts (attached/linked)
- Attached: benchmarks-snapshot-20270615.csv (cost/latency/quality metrics across our candidate slate)
- Prompt pack: summit-prompt-pack-v1.zip — includes sample user prompts, canonical expected outputs, and evaluation scripts for ROUGE/BERTSim/QA accuracy checks
- Demo recording: redwood-demo-recording-20270615.mp4 (internal)
- Eval dashboard: https://redwood-internal.company/eval-dashboard/SUM-2027-084 (interactive charts and per-prompt traces)

Quality/regression risks and mitigations
- Medium-quant variant shows a ~2–3% drop in QA accuracy on the hardest prompts (legal/technical language). Mitigation: keep a small ruler set of ~200 high-risk prompts from your corpus and run nightly regression checks; promote to fallback if drop >5%.
- For long-document summarization (>2k tokens) prefer open-xl-13B-8bit or rw-xl-hq; the medium-quant option can be used with chunking + KV cache aggregation but expect coherence issues without prompt engineering.

Operational recommendations
- Canary rollout: route 10% traffic to rw-xl-hq for first 48 hours after go-live for high-value customers; monitor latency tail and model outputs via our tracing hooks.
- Autoscale: configure burst capacity for peak usage windows — we can pre-warm 2 dedicated workers for Summit’s initial traffic pattern.
- Cost control: set a soft token cap per session and a hard cap across the account; Redwood Optimize suggestions included in the Drive folder.

Next steps (proposed timeline)
1) Agree on objective metrics (by EOD Thu): target QA accuracy, ROUGE thresholds, acceptable P95 latency.
2) Redwood to hand over prompt pack + a short runbook for the two-track eval (today — attached).
3) Summit to confirm which internal S3 / GCP bucket we should write logs to; we can send ingestion config for automatic dashboarding (by Fri).
4) Schedule a 30-min sync next week to review initial 48-hour results and adjust routing.

Action items
- Samira: confirm metrics and log bucket (reply)
- Summit Tech Ops: flag any firewall/egress rules that would block our collector (cc’ed)
- Marissa/Karthik: I’ll assign the eval runner and share a run command + Dockerfile for your infra team.

Thanks — happy to jump on a call if you want to walk through the benchmark CSV and the prompt pack.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: summit-prompt-pack-v1.zip (prompt pack, README, eval scripts); benchmarks-snapshot-20270615.csv (benchmarks summary)

Cc: Summit Tech Ops <engops@summit.dev>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Suggested inference engines & prioritized shortlist — Summit demo follow-up

Thanks Ben — this is super helpful and aligns with what we talked about. A few quick Qs from our engineering lead: 

1) Can you share expected monthly cost estimates for the three options at our projected volume (50M tokens/month)?
2) For the medium-quant option, how often would we expect to retrain or refresh the prompt sets to avoid quality drift?
3) Any compliance considerations if we keep logs in our GCP bucket vs using Redwood-managed retention?

We’re okay to start the 2-week run — Samir (eng lead) will confirm infra details by EOD and I’ll cc the billing contact if we need an initial cost estimate. Also appreciate the canary suggestion — that works for our rollout plan.

—Samira
Samira Khan
Head of Product 
Summit Analytics

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@summit.dev>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Summit Tech Ops <engops@summit.dev>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 18:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Suggested inference engines & prioritized shortlist — Summit demo follow-up

Samira — quick answers inline and next steps.

1) Cost estimate (50M tokens/month) — rough monthly TCO (hosted API, blended):
   - redwood-rw-medium-quant: ~$1,800/month (blended per-token + infra)
   - open-xl-13B-8bit: ~$3,900/month
   - redwood-rw-xl-hq: ~$7,200/month
   Notes: these are estimates for typical Summit traffic pattern (mix of short chat and occasional long summaries). I’ll ask finance to produce a formal quote if you want committed reserved capacity pricing.

2) Prompt-set refresh cadence for medium-quant: start with weekly checks against the 200 high-risk prompt ruler. If you see systematic drift (>3% QA accuracy drop) we recommend rolling an updated prompt pack and running a short A/B (5% traffic) for validation. Over time cadence can go to biweekly/monthly depending on stability.

3) Compliance/logs: keeping logs in your GCP bucket is preferred for regulated customers — Redwood can push logs to your bucket and we do not store PII in our long-term retention unless explicitly configured. We’ll provide the ingestion config and an IAM role suggestion for secure write-only access. If you prefer Redwood-managed retention we offer SOC 2 enclaves for higher retention and audit reporting (this incurs an additional fee).

Next concrete items from our side:
- Marissa: can you share the runner docker command and the small eval job (Karthik will own infra hints) so Summit can kick off tomorrow?
- Ben: I’ll loop finance for a formal estimate and a reserved capacity option if Summit is interested in predictable unit costs.
- Samira/EngOps: send the bucket name and any firewall egress blocks; we’ll verify the collector works within 24h.

If helpful I can schedule a 30min check-in Fri morning PST to confirm the runbook and telemetry hooks.

Best,
Ben

Marissa Cole (on behalf of Redwood team)

- Shortlist (priority order): 1) redwood-rw-medium-quant (cost-efficient, strong latency for chat), 2) open-xl-13B-8bit (balanced quality/cost), 3) redwood-rw-xl-hq (highest quality, higher cost — use as fallback).

Why these three?
- redwood-rw-medium-quant: ~1.8x cost improvement vs unfused 8-bit XL on our benchmarks for short chats (<=512 tokens) with negligible quality regression in instruction-following. Median P95 latency ~110ms on our dedicated instance. Ideal to serve interactive chat at scale.
- open-xl-13B-8bit: more consistent on longer contexts (1k-2k tokens) with modest latency increase (~220ms P95) and noticeably better summarization coherence than the medium quant variant. Good middle ground if you want a single engine to cover both flows.
- redwood-rw-xl-hq: best-in-class for multi-page summarization and tricky context disambiguation. Cost is ~3-4x the medium quant option per token; use as a canary/fallback for expensive jobs or critical customer flows.

Benchmarks and artifacts (attached/linked)
- Attached: benchmarks-snapshot-20270615.csv (cost/latency/quality metrics across our candidate slate)
- Prompt pack: summit-prompt-pack-v1.zip — includes sample user prompts, canonical expected outputs, and evaluation scripts for ROUGE/BERTSim/QA accuracy checks
- Demo recording: redwood-demo-recording-20270615.mp4 (internal)
- Eval dashboard: https://redwood-internal.company/eval-dashboard/SUM-2027-084 (interactive charts and per-prompt traces)

Quality/regression risks and mitigations
- Medium-quant variant shows a ~2–3% drop in QA accuracy on the hardest prompts (legal/technical language). Mitigation: keep a small ruler set of ~200 high-risk prompts from your corpus and run nightly regression checks; promote to fallback if drop >5%.
- For long-document summarization (>2k tokens) prefer open-xl-13B-8bit or rw-xl-hq; the medium-quant option can be used with chunking + KV cache aggregation but expect coherence issues without prompt engineering.

Operational recommendations
- Canary rollout: route 10% traffic to rw-xl-hq for first 48 hours after go-live for high-value customers; monitor latency tail and model outputs via our tracing hooks.
- Autoscale: configure burst capacity for peak usage windows — we can pre-warm 2 dedicated workers for Summit’s initial traffic pattern.
- Cost control: set a soft token cap per session and a hard cap across the account; Redwood Optimize suggestions included in the Drive folder.

Next steps (proposed timeline)
1) Agree on objective metrics (by EOD Thu): target QA accuracy, ROUGE thresholds, acceptable P95 latency.
2) Redwood to hand over prompt pack + a short runbook for the two-track eval (today — attached).
3) Summit to confirm which internal S3 / GCP bucket we should write logs to; we can send ingestion config for automatic dashboarding (by Fri).
4) Schedule a 30-min sync next week to review initial 48-hour results and adjust routing.

Action items
- Samira: confirm metrics and log bucket (reply)
- Summit Tech Ops: flag any firewall/egress rules that would block our collector (cc’ed)
- Marissa/Karthik: I’ll assign the eval runner and share a run command + Dockerfile for your infra team.

Thanks — happy to jump on a call if you want to walk through the benchmark CSV and the prompt pack.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0198

   - open-xl-13B-8bit: ~$3,900/month

title: Request: sustain sandbox while engineering unblocks integration
preview:
From: Miguel Torres <miguel@fieldlytics.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Trial access - integration stalled

Hi Grace / Michael,

Quick status from our side: our engineering team hit a serialization bug in the adapter that prevents payloads from reaching Redwood's onboarding webhook. They've been triaging since Friday but are blocked by a dependency upgrade that surfaced unexpected schema changes.

Because of this we won't be able to complete the POC integration by the end of our trial window (June 21). We really like Redwood's latency and the dedicated routing demo — the execs want to see the connector run end-to-end before we make a decision. Can you grant an extension on the sandbox and a small top-up of credits so our engineers can continue testing while we fix the adapter? I attached the integration logs that show the serialization trace.

Rough ask: extend trial to July 12 and add ~5k sandbox tokens/credits. Happy to hop on a short call today to walk through the logs.

Thanks,
Miguel Torres
Senior Platform Engineer | Fieldlytics
attachment: integration-log.zip (9.2 MB)


From: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Miguel Torres <miguel@fieldlytics.com>
Cc: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 09:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Trial access - integration stalled

Hi Miguel — thanks for the detail and for sending the logs. I'm sorry to hear you've been blocked; that kind of adapter issue is exactly the sort of thing we want to clear quickly.

I'm looping in our infra lead David and your AE Ben so we can (1) triage the logs, (2) confirm a short-term credits/top-up, and (3) propose an extension that keeps your pilot moving.

Next steps:
- David, can you review the attached logs and confirm whether this is on the Fieldlytics adapter side or something we should handle on the webhook parsing?
- Ben, please prepare an interim credit proposal we can approve quickly if the technical ask checks out.

Miguel — we should be able to sort a short extension; will follow up today with specifics. If you can share a narrow test payload that reproduces the error, that would speed things up.

Best,
Grace O'Connor
Director, Customer Success | Redwood Inference
redwood.com


From: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, Miguel Torres <miguel@fieldlytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Trial access - integration stalled

On Mon, Jun 14, 2027 at 09:12 AM Miguel Torres <miguel@fieldlytics.com> wrote:
> Because of this we won't be able to complete the POC integration by the end of our trial window (June 21).

Thanks for the logs, Miguel. I pulled the serialization trace — the failure shows non-UTF8 control characters in an embedded metadata field. That's coming from the adapter transformation step (Fieldlytics side) rather than the webhook parser. The good news: the fix is straightforward (sanitize control chars before JSON encode) and your engineer can implement a patch.

Estimated timeline if prioritized: 48-72 hours for a patch and internal verification. If you need additional runway beyond June 21 while your team fixes and re-tests, I'd recommend we:
- Extend the sandbox through July 12,
- Add a 5k sandbox credit top-up (applied to your trial account), and
- Schedule a quick validation call the day you deploy the patch so we can confirm end-to-end.

I'll leave the credit approval to Ben/Grace but confirm this is a technical adapter issue and the above timeline is realistic.

— David
David Kim
Senior Reliability Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>

- Notes: credits applied immediately; extension applied in HubSpot and billing console.

Ben Carter

We're kicking off a SOC 2 + SIG evidence pull for an internal audit and Nimbus needs demonstrable extracts of admin activity and related audit logs. Can you confirm the following and, if possible, provide a delivery option by EOD next Tuesday (2027-06-21):

1) Canonical list of events you log that are relevant to our controls (admin console changes, user provisioning, role changes, API token issuance/revocation, key/KMS operations, model deploys, data export events, authentication / SSO successes/failures, IP addresses and user agents, request IDs).

2) Retention policy for each event class and whether there is a cold/archived store beyond the standard window.

3) Export options: ad-hoc snapshot, scheduled export to our S3 bucket, streaming to SIEM, presigned download URLs, or a secure transfer with chain-of-custody manifest. For each option we need expected time-to-deliver and checksum/manifest details.

4) Any PII redaction or masking applied to logs, and whether a full unredacted set can be delivered to a designated security mailbox under an NDA.

Taylor Reed
Senior InfoSec Manager, Nimbus Bank
security@nimbusbank.com



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Taylor Reed <taylor.reed@nimbusbank.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evidence request: admin activity and audit log extracts (SOC 2 + SIG) — Nimbus Bank

Hi Taylor — thanks for the clear list. Quick ack — we can provide everything you requested with a few options depending on scope and sensitivity. Below is an overview and recommended default for SOC 2/SIG evidence pulls.

What we log (high level):
- admin_activity: user/agent identity, action (create/update/delete), target resource, timestamp, request_id, IP, user_agent, region
- operator_trail: full operator calls (API payloads for control plane operations) — stored as redacted blobs by default; full blobs available under escrow/NDA for certain customers
- auth_events: SSO/OAuth token issuance, refresh, revocation, MFA events, failed logins
- config_changes: model rollouts, routing policy changes, quota changes, key rotations (KMS events)
- data_access: embedding/response access logs (model, prompt_id, tokens_count, caller_id)
- system_health: service restarts, node reassignments (typically lower fidelity; included only if requested)

Default retention (our hosted prod):
- Hot audit logs: 90 days (searchable in Console)
- Cold / archived logs: 365 days retained in compressed archive (S3 standard IA for 1 year)
- Operator trail escrow: 7 years (measure for regulated customers; can be extended via Private deployments)

Export/delivery options (summary):
A) Ad-hoc snapshot (recommended for point-in-time SOC evidence) — we generate an export bundle (gzip per-table) + manifest (SHA256) and provide a presigned S3 URL. Typical turnaround: same day for small datasets (<10GB), 24-48h for larger.
B) Scheduled push to customer S3 (preferred for recurring audits) — daily/hourly objects to a customer-owned bucket with per-file manifest and checksum. Initial delivery/setup 2–4 business days.
C) Streaming to SIEM (syslog/HTTPS) — near-real-time; requires customer endpoint and validated TLS.
D) BigQuery/Log sink export — for customers who want analytics-ready exports.
E) Secure courier (rare) — physical drive + chain-of-custody for extreme regulatory needs.

Security & integrity: every bundle includes a machine-readable manifest (file paths, SHA256, byte counts, generation timestamp, request_id). We can sign manifests with an HSM-backed key or provide PGP-encrypted bundles on request.

Attachments: I attached a quick log inventory and an export-options matrix to help you pick.

Next steps: please confirm which export option you prefer and whether Nimbus requires an unredacted operator trail under NDA. Once confirmed we'll schedule the snapshot and provide the presigned URL or start configuration for scheduled exports.

Best,
Ben Carter
Director, Security Solutions — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0132

[attachment: export_options_matrix.pdf]

title: CarePoint: SIG/HIPAA — model-training exclusion & transcript retention
preview:
From: Lindsay Harper <lindsay.harper@carepointhealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@carepointhealth.com>; Dr. Rajiv Menon <rajiv.menon@carepointhealth.com>
Date: 2027-06-13T14:02:00-07:00
Subject: Clarify model-training use of CarePoint data + HIPAA evidence request

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week. Before we move to procurement I need to clear a couple of items with security and legal. Our questions are focused on the AI/LLM privacy posture for patient-facing workflows. Specifically:

- Do you ever use customer-provided prompts, files, or generated responses to further train or fine-tune your models (weights or datasets) for hosted Redwood models? If yes, under what contractual options can CarePoint ensure our data is never used for training?
- What is your default transcript/log retention for API calls and streaming sessions? Can retention be reduced to zero or fully disabled for an enterprise contract?
- For HIPAA-regulated data, can you outline the evidence you can provide (SOC 2 summary, SIG/CAIQ, BAA) and an example data-flow diagram showing where PHI is stored and who has access?

Procurement also needs any attachments you can share for the security review (SOC2 summary, SIG spreadsheet, a HIPAA/BAA template). Can you send a one-page attestation stating whether Redwood will explicitly exclude our data from model training for the configuration we discussed?

Appreciate a quick turnaround — procurement would like to include this in the SOW by end of next week.

Thanks,
Lindsay Harper
Director, AI Integrations
CarePoint Health



From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lindsay Harper <lindsay.harper@carepointhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-14T09:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Clarify model-training use of CarePoint data + HIPAA evidence request

Lindsay — thanks for the clear list, and great to meet you last week. Looping in Ben (security) and Monica (solutions) so we can provide formal artifacts. High level answers while they gather the attachments: 

- Training on customer data: For our Hosted API tier, customer content is protected but may be used to improve models by default unless a contractual \"no-derivative training\" clause and opt-out are added. For Redwood Dedicated (reserved GPU pools) and Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem), customer data is not used for model weight updates — we provide an explicit non-training attestation in those deployment modes.

- Retention: Default retention for API logs in Hosted is 30 days for troubleshooting and abuse prevention; we offer configurable retention down to 0 days for enterprise customers (short-term transient storage or immediate deletion) as part of an advanced configuration and SOW. Dedicated/Private deployments give you full control over storage lifetime.

- HIPAA evidence: We can provide SOC 2 Type II summary, completed SIG SAQ responses, a HIPAA BAA template, and a data-flow diagram showing where PHI is processed and stored in each deployment mode.

Ben — can you attach the SOC2 summary, the SIG spreadsheet, and a one-page attestation draft (no-train language for Dedicated/Private and contractual option text for Hosted)? Monica — can you include a short procurement checklist and the example data-flow SVG?

We can get a draft attestation and the listed artifacts to you by end of day tomorrow. If procurement prefers, we can route the BAA via DocuSign for signature.

Best,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Account Exec, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
(415) 555-0187



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lindsay Harper <lindsay.harper@carepointhealth.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-14T16:46:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Clarify model-training use of CarePoint data + HIPAA evidence request

Lindsay — following up with the technical details and attached artifacts (see attachments). Short summary: 

1) Model training/use of customer data
- Hosted API: by default we collect telemetry and usage metadata for quality and safety; raw prompts/responses are not used to update model weights unless a customer opts into \"contribute-to-model-improvements\" program. We offer a contractual opt-out clause that prohibits any use of customer content for model training or derivative model development.
- Dedicated & Private: customer data is isolated; we do not use prompts, files, or generated outputs to retrain or fine-tune shared models. When deployed in Dedicated/Private, you control storage and can disable telemetry collection.

2) Transcript/log retention and access
- Default hosted retention: 30 days (encrypted at rest, access controls via RBAC, and audit logging).
- Enterprise options: retention configurable to 0–365 days; we can implement immediate deletion at the app level or store only anonymized telemetry.
- Access: only named SRE and Security engineers have tooling-level access; access is audited and requires approvals.

3) HIPAA evidence and contractual items
- SOC 2 Type II summary: attached (Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_summary_2026.pdf).
- SIG/CAIQ: attached spreadsheet with completed SAQ items (Redwood_SIG_SAQS_Responses_2027.xlsx).
- BAA: attached HIPAA_BAA_template_2027.docx — we can route via DocuSign (link below) once procurement confirms signatories.
- Data flow diagram: attached (dataflow_llm_private_dedicated.svg) showing separation between hosted telemetry and customer-controlled storage for Dedicated/Private.

4) Attestation language (preview)
- For Dedicated/Private deployments: \"Redwood Inference attests that customer-provided content (prompts, files, and generated outputs) will not be used to train, fine-tune, or derive model weights or training datasets absent explicit customer consent.\"
- For Hosted deployments: contractual no-training opt-out clause is available; by default, hosted data may be used for product improvements unless opted out in writing.

Next steps: Ava/Monica and I can produce a one-page signed attestation and a procurement checklist. I also uploaded the artifacts to a gated Drive folder and have included the DocuSign BAA link below.

Attachments (included): Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_summary_2026.pdf; Redwood_SIG_SAQS_Responses_2027.xlsx; HIPAA_BAA_template_2027.docx; dataflow_llm_private_dedicated.svg

Links:
- Evidence folder: https://drive.redwoodinference.com/shared/secure-llm-evidence
- DocuSign (BAA): https://docusign.com/BAA/Redwood/1234

If you'd like, we can provide a short redline-friendly BAA and the signed attestation within 24 hours of procurement confirmation.

Thanks,

- Training on customer data: For our Hosted API tier, customer content is protected but may be used to improve models by default unless a contractual \"no-derivative training\" clause and opt-out are added. Dedicated/Private deployments give you full control over storage lifetime.

- HIPAA evidence: We can provide SOC 2 Type II summary, completed SIG SAQ responses, a HIPAA BAA template, and a data-flow diagram showing where PHI is processed and stored in each deployment mode.

title: Capacity certify runway & swap orchestration for Pioneer Health migration
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Matthew Smith <matthew.smith@pioneerhealth.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Capacity certify runway & swap orchestration for Pioneer Health migration

Matthew —

Thanks again for the walkthrough today. As promised, I pulled together a suggested certification and swap runway so we can validate the dedicated capacity you contracted and land the hosted->dedicated swap with minimal disruption. High level: we want to (1) certify the committed GPU bank under your peak slice, (2) exercise warm-standby routing and KV cache priming, (3) run a staged quiesce and swap during a low-traffic window.

Attached: pioneer_capacity_model_v3.xlsx (capacity pulse & expected token/sec), certify_runway_checklist.docx (gates).

Key asks for Pioneer: please confirm the accepted acceptance thresholds below by EOD Friday:
- P95 latency on 4k context <= 380 ms
- Throughput target: 1.2M tokens/hr sustained for burst window (60m)
- Error rate <= 0.2% during stress test

Proposed timeline (draft):
- 6/11: Capacity provisioning & model variant pin (dev/staging)
- 6/12: Stress run + KV cache priming (off-hours)
- 6/13: Canary traffic ramp (10% -> 50%)
- 6/14: Final swap in 2-hour quiesce window (02:00–04:00 local)

I've looped Karthik and Grace to scope the technical validation steps and runbook. If that looks good, we'll circulate a formal mutual action plan and the DocuSign for the usage amendment.

Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment stub: pioneer_capacity_model_v3.xlsx (xlsx, 42KB)
Attachment stub: certify_runway_checklist.docx (docx, 18KB)


From: Matthew Smith <matthew.smith@pioneerhealth.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@pioneerhealth.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 12:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Capacity certify runway & swap orchestration for Pioneer Health migration

Ben —

Appreciate the clear plan. Procurement is reviewing the usage amendment (CC'd). Quick answers to your asks:
- Latency: Pioneer is targeting P95 <= 400 ms for the first production week; 380 ms is OK as an aspirational gate.
- Throughput: we'll confirm final QPS numbers on 6/11 after we pull production traces, but 1.2M tokens/hr burst looks aligned with our commit.
- Error rate: agreed <= 0.2% for the stress test.

One ask from our side: we need a warm-standby pool to absorb a 2x short-term spike during canary escalation (i.e., ability to route to hosted fallback for up to 15% of traffic). Can you confirm Redwood's fallback routing policy and expected failover latency?

I'll aim to confirm trace numbers by 6/11 morning.

—Matthew
Matthew Smith
Head of ML Ops, Pioneer Health


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Matthew Smith <matthew.smith@pioneerhealth.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 11 Jun 2027 07:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Capacity certify runway & swap orchestration for Pioneer Health migration

Morning —

Short answers on fallback & gates, plus technical notes for the checklist:
- Fallback routing: we support policy-driven routing. For this plan we'll configure a warm-standby fallback to Redwood Hosted with automatic failover when dedicated latency exceeds the P95 gate or when error rate >0.5% for a sustained 3min window. Failover detection -> reroute should happen within ~8–12s for single-request routing; bulk retries may extend effective failover slippage (we'll test).

- Warm-standby sizing: to support a 2x short-term spike on the canary pool, we recommend reserving an additional 20% GPU headroom in the warm-standby pool. That equates to +4 A100-equivalent GPUs for the committed bank (see pioneer_capacity_model_v3.xlsx, 'WarmPool' tab).

- KV cache priming: to minimize cold-start token cost and latency flares during the ramp, plan a 90-minute prefix replay session against staging with production traces (we can run this on 6/12 UTC 01:00).

- Stress test config: 3x synthetic concurrency for 30m followed by 60m sustained target; collect full perf profile (latency percentiles, kernel utilization, memory fragmentation).

Action items for Pioneer: share a 24hr trace slice and the top-20 prompt templates by 6/11 12:00 UTC so Grace and I can finalize perf-params.

Karthik
Principal Infra Eng, Redwood Inference

---
Attachment stub: stress_config.json (json, 2KB)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Matthew Smith <matthew.smith@pioneerhealth.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 12 Jun 2027 10:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Capacity certify runway & swap orchestration for Pioneer Health migration

Matthew —

Thanks — received your confirmation on gates. Practical next steps:
- Pioneer to upload the 24hr trace and top templates to the Drive link (we shared) by 6/11 noon UTC.
- Redwood will run the KV priming on 6/12 01:00 UTC and the stress run immediately after.
- We'll produce a short 'certify' report and a go/no-go scorecard by 6/12 10:00 UTC.

Evelyn (solutions engineering) is copied to coordinate the DocuSign amendment once Procurement signs off. Grace — can your ops lead own the quiesce/swap window checklist and confirm the 02:00–04:00 local window on 6/14?

Ben

On a separate note, Procurement asked about a 90-day SLA exception for an earlier cutover window; Evelyn, I left that to you to escalate if needed.

---
Attachment stub: cutover_playbook_v1.pdf (pdf, 120KB)


From: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Matthew Smith <matthew.smith@pioneerhealth.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 17:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Finalized certify-runway, swap timeline and operator playbook

Team —

We completed the staging stress run overnight and the KV priming completed without errors. Summary highlights from the certify report (attached to drive link):
- Observed P95 on 4k context: 372 ms (meets 380 ms gate)
- Sustained throughput at target: averaged 1.25M tokens/hr for the 60-minute window
- Error rate: 0.09% during stress window
- Failover test: warm-standby reroute occurred in ~9s; redispatch retry tail latency was within acceptable bounds

Given the results, we are green to proceed with the swap on 2027-06-14 in the 02:00–04:00 local quiesce window. Below is the operator timeline for the window:
- 01:30: Reduce ingress to canary pool to 10%
- 01:45: Snapshot KV cache and begin prefix replay for final priming (30m)
- 02:00: Enter quiesce window; disable scheduled background jobs and pause autoscaling
- 02:10: Swap traffic to Dedicated bank (step ramp: 25% -> 50% -> 100% over 40m)
- 02:55: Run smoke suite (end-to-end token checks, auth, tracing)
- 03:30: Stand up hosted fallback at 15% routing as contingency; monitor for 30m
- 04:00: Declare swap complete and resume normal autoscaling

Operator notes: if error rate >0.3% or P95 climbs > 455 ms during ramp, revert to snapshot and fallback to hosted for up to 60m, then re-evaluate.

Matthew — please confirm you and Procurement are good with the window; once confirmed, Evelyn will issue the DocuSign amendment and we'll schedule one final ops rehearsal for 6/13 22:00 UTC.

Thanks,
Grace
Sr. Customer Reliability Lead, Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ellen Park <ellen.park@brightbridgehealth.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@brightbridgehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Stakeholder roster + systems scope — follow-up & security kickoff

Thanks again for the demo earlier today — appreciate the time. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth: 

Recap
- Primary use case: inline recommendations inside patient portal and an offline analytics scoring pipeline.
- Tech contacts: Ellen (security/architecture) + Tom (product lead).
- Procurement + legal: Sana (procurement) and Michael Chen (VP Finance) are decision-makers on terms and PO.
- Timeline: goal to complete security intake and procurement review by end of Q3 (your org) to start a pilot in Q4.

Open questions I captured
1) Confirm the systems in scope for the security review (are we limited to patient portal + analytics, or is the ETL layer / data lake also in scope?)

3) Preferred format for the questionnaire — PDF download vs. shared Google form.

Attachments / links
- I’ve attached the Redwood security questionnaire (PDF) for you to review; we can accept a partially completed copy if you want to iterate.
- HubSpot link to the account record: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/company/BBH-2027-019

Proposed next steps (short and actionable)
- Ellen: confirm in-scope systems and whether ETL/data lake are included (reply inline).
- Tom / Michael: confirm procurement timeline and whether PO/standard SOW will be required.
- BrightBridge procurement: can you nominate the procurement contact who will receive the contract & vendor form? (Sana listed?)
- From Redwood: Marissa will draft an architecture MVP diagram and Rishi will prepare initial answers for the questionnaire sections related to encryption, retention, and SOC2 evidence.

If that sounds right, we’ll aim to turn around the first-pass questionnaire responses within 3 business days after you confirm scope.

Thanks —

Subject: Draft: short DPA / transfer blurb + sales talktrack for Hosted API

Hi Sophie —

Thanks again for the time last week. Per our call, procurement wanted a short, customer-facing paragraph explaining how Redwood handles cross-border transfers and subprocessors for the Hosted API (something a procurement team can paste into a sourcing questionnaire). I pulled together two options and a one-line talktrack for your buyer funnel. See attached: DPA_snippets_v1.docx.

Quick notes on the two options in the doc:
- Option A (2-3 lines): concise consumer-friendly blurb — simpler and better for marketing-facing RFPs.
- Option B (bullet list): a little more explicit on subprocessors, transfer mechanisms (SCCs / EU transfers), and where to find the full subprocessor list in Console.

Ask: Can you share which tone Procurement prefers (short vs. explicit), and whether Legal wants the paragraph to reference specific transfer mechanisms by name or keep it generic? If Legal wants specifics we can add a parenthetical pointing to our transfer safeguards page.

Attachments: DPA_snippets_v1.docx (docx, ~7KB)

Thanks — I’ll update the snippet once we’ve agreed on tone and share a final copy for your procurement/contracting team to use.

— Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Cc: Mike Reyes <mike.reyes@acme.com>

Ben — thanks for the draft. I had Procurement and Legal take a quick look. Procurement prefers Option A (short), but Legal is asking for two clarifications before we can include the blurb in our standard vendor questionnaire:

1) When you say "standard cross-border safeguards," can you specify which mechanism(s) you use for EU transfers (e.g., SCCs or adequacy)?
2) Where exactly will the subprocessor list live (Console link or public page) — we need a persistent URL our contract team can reference.

If you can add a one-line clarifying parenthetical for the transfer mechanism and a stable link to the subprocessor list, I think Legal will be comfortable. Also attaching our MAAP questions that came up so you know the context.

Thanks — Sophie

> Ben — thanks for the draft. I had Procurement and Legal take a quick look...

Thanks for the ping — quick legal redline suggestions below. I focused on language that is buyer-friendly but does not overcommit beyond what we have in our DPA and Console.

Proposed edits for Option A (single-line blurb):
- Keep it short, but explicitly reference the primary transfer mechanism: "We rely on EU Commission standard contractual clauses (SCCs) or other lawful transfer mechanisms as applicable."
- Link to a stable Console page for the subprocessor list and add phrase: "a current list of subprocessors is available in the Redwood Console (link)."

Do not include guarantees about specific hosting geographies beyond what the customer configures — we can say "customers can choose regional residency where offered."

I’ve updated the attached doc with tracked changes and a one-line legal-approved parenthetical. Ben — if you want, I can also produce a single-sentence 'procurement-ready' snippet labeled as "Legal-approved (for procurement)" that you can insert into Acme's questionnaire.

—Kimberly

title: Dedicated footprint sequencing & vendor timing — ACME AI
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena@quantaprocure.com>, Raj Patel <raj.patel@cloudcorp.com>, procurement@acmeai.com
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-10T09:12:00Z
Subject: Dedicated footprint sequencing & vendor timing — ACME AI

Team / partners — initiating a single coordination thread to lock timing and procurement sequencing for the ACME AI Dedicated rollout. High level context: ACME signed a reserved-capacity deal last week (ACME-DED-2027). Their requested production ramp is staged: initial block for Q3 (target live date ~2027-09-01) with two additional cluster tranches by end of Q4. We need an aligned procurement plan that minimizes customer lead-time risk while controlling cost.

Key asks in this email:
- Elena / Quanta: confirm lead-time bands for H100 / A100-equivalent SKUs and whether you can provide partial shipments (e.g., early 30% drop-ship) and staging pool allocation. Please include any volume discounts or allocation prioritization windows.
- Raj / CloudCorp: confirm any short-term reserved instance (RI) or committed-use options we can route as a cloud backfill (region: us-west1) while hardware arrives. Include expected ramp-up provisioning time once PO is issued.
- Internal (Karthik / Marissa / Jonas): please review the attached capacity worksheet and SOW draft, and flag any requirements that would change SKU counts or schedule (network, rack power, cooling).

Proposed plan for discussion:
1) Lock a PO for the first tranche (30% capacity) as soon as we have a binding quote and ETA (target by 2027-06-20).
2) Add conditional POs for tranche 2 and 3 with agreed laddered shipment dates and cancellation/roll-forward terms.
3) Use CloudCorp reserved capacity to backfill up to 40% of shortfall in the first 90 days post go-live; ramp down as hardware arrives.

Attachments: ACME_capacity_requirements.xlsx (spread of cluster sizes / rack counts), SOW_draft_v0.9.pdf

Open timeline/questions:
- What are concrete ETA ranges (weeks) for full shipments vs partial shipments?
- Will Quanta hold allocation for a PO signature or only upon full payment?
- Any lead-time variance by serial/batch that we should account for?

Please reply with concrete ETA windows and any requirements for our PO terms. Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Director, Strategic Partnerships | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0132



From: Elena Soto <elena@quantaprocure.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Raj Patel <raj.patel@cloudcorp.com>, procurement@acmeai.com
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-11T14:30:00Z
Subject: Re: Dedicated footprint sequencing & vendor timing — ACME AI

Hi Ben / team — thanks for the clear ask. Quick summary from Quanta on inventory and timing:

- SKU availability: We can confirm access to H100-class units for the US region. Standard lead-time is 10–12 weeks from PO for full factory allocation under current queueing.
- Partial shipments: We can schedule a staggered delivery model. First partial shipment (approx 30% of ordered units) can be delivered in ~7–8 weeks after PO if we gate on a non-refundable deposit and sign an allocation hold. Subsequent shipments follow the 10–12 week horizon for the remaining units.
- Allocation hold: We will reserve allocation upon signed PO + 30% deposit or a binding purchase commitment (our preferred route). Without deposit, we can provide a quote but cannot guarantee the allocation window.
- Staging pool: We have a limited pool of pre-configured nodes we can drop-ship for short-term staging (up to 20% of the full order) with a 4–6 week lead-time; pricing is a nominal uplift (see attached quote).

Attached: Quanta_Quote_0611.pdf (contains SKU-level pricing, partial-shipment schedule, T&Cs). A few notes from the quote:
- Freight & customs are estimated separately; domestic US deliveries will be DDP in most regions but confirm your data-center addresses.
- We support cross-shipment by serial batches to allow progressive rack installation.

Options to reduce risk if ACME needs earlier capacity:
1) Hybrid: sign PO for tranche 1 + staging pool drop-ship for earliest traffic.
2) Cloud backfill: we can coordinate with CloudCorp to hold staging nodes while hardware is staged.
3) Alternate SKUs: if customer accepts performance delta, we can offer A100-equivalent faster delivery (~5–7 weeks) at modest cost savings.

Next steps we need from Redwood / ACME to firm this up:
- Confirmation of the tranche sizes (attached workbook helps).
- Acceptance of our PO deposit terms or an alternative commitment instrument.
- Final delivery addresses and rack power profiles.

Happy to hop on a short call tomorrow to walk the quote if helpful.

Best — Elena

[Attachment: Quanta_Quote_0611.pdf (application/pdf)]

On Fri, Jun 11, 2027 at 02:30 PM Elena Soto <elena@quantaprocure.com> wrote:
> Ben / team — sending the formal quote attached as discussed.
>


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Soto <elena@quantaprocure.com>, Raj Patel <raj.patel@cloudcorp.com>
Date: 2027-06-12T10:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Dedicated footprint sequencing & vendor timing — ACME AI

Ben / team — quick internal alignment notes after reading Quanta's reply.

Inline:
> Partial shipments: first ~30% in 7–8 weeks; remainder 10–12 weeks.

Risk profile: this is acceptable if we have a firm cloud backfill plan for the first 60–90 days after go-live. If the customer expects production traffic day one at target SLA, we need to size the backfill to cover peak or negotiate a ramped SLA with ACME.

Recommendations:
- Accept H100 partial shipment for tranche 1, but negotiate a contractual service credit tied to delivery slippage for tranches 2/3. Add a clause that moves tranche 3 delivery + credit if Quanta misses the agreed date by >14 days.
- Short-term: request Raj to provision CloudCorp reserved nodes as a non-disruptive backfill. Ask for a 2–4 week activation SLA after we issue a cloud reservation PO.
- Consider a blended baseline: start with 70% native hardware + 30% CloudCorp backfill for Q3, then move to 100% native by Q4. This lowers immediate capital and reduces exposure.

Action items:

Key asks in this email:
- Elena / Quanta: confirm lead-time bands for H100 / A100-equivalent SKUs and whether you can provide partial shipments (e.g., early 30% drop-ship) and staging pool allocation. Please include any volume discounts or allocation prioritization windows.

2) Add conditional POs for tranche 2 and 3 with agreed laddered shipment dates and cancellation/roll-forward terms.
3) Use CloudCorp reserved capacity to backfill up to 40% of shortfall in the first 90 days post go-live; ramp down as hardware arrives.

Attachments: ACME_capacity_requirements.xlsx (spread of cluster sizes / rack counts), SOW_draft_v0.9.pdf

Open timeline/questions:
- What are concrete ETA ranges (weeks) for full shipments vs partial shipments?
- Will Quanta hold allocation for a PO signature or only upon full payment?
- Any lead-time variance by serial/batch that we should account for?

From: Elena Soto <elena@quantaprocure.com>

Date: 2027-06-11T14:30:00Z
Subject: Re: Dedicated footprint sequencing & vendor timing — ACME AI

Hi Ben / team — thanks for the clear ask. Quick summary from Quanta on inventory and timing:

- SKU availability: We can confirm access to H100-class units for the US region. Standard lead-time is 10–12 weeks from PO for full factory allocation under current queueing.
- Partial shipments: We can schedule a staggered delivery model. First partial shipment (approx 30% of ordered units) can be delivered in ~7–8 weeks after PO if we gate on a non-refundable deposit and sign an allocation hold. Subsequent shipments follow the 10–12 week horizon for the remaining units.
- Allocation hold: We will reserve allocation upon signed PO + 30% deposit or a binding purchase commitment (our preferred route). Without deposit, we can provide a quote but cannot guarantee the allocation window.
- Staging pool: We have a limited pool of pre-configured nodes we can drop-ship for short-term staging (up to 20% of the full order) with a 4–6 week lead-time; pricing is a nominal uplift (see attached quote).

Attached: Quanta_Quote_0611.pdf (contains SKU-level pricing, partial-shipment schedule, T&Cs). A few notes from the quote:
- Freight & customs are estimated separately; domestic US deliveries will be DDP in most regions but confirm your data-center addresses.
- We support cross-shipment by serial batches to allow progressive rack installation.

Options to reduce risk if ACME needs earlier capacity:
1) Hybrid: sign PO for tranche 1 + staging pool drop-ship for earliest traffic.
2) Cloud backfill: we can coordinate with CloudCorp to hold staging nodes while hardware is staged.
3) Alternate SKUs: if customer accepts performance delta, we can offer A100-equivalent faster delivery (~5–7 weeks) at modest cost savings.

Next steps we need from Redwood / ACME to firm this up:
- Confirmation of the tranche sizes (attached workbook helps).
- Acceptance of our PO deposit terms or an alternative commitment instrument.
- Final delivery addresses and rack power profiles.

Happy to hop on a short call tomorrow to walk the quote if helpful.

Best — Elena

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@acmeco.com>, Tim Reed <tim.reed@acmeco.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 9 Jun 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: MSA + Order Form redlines — DPA/SCCs delegation and signature gate

Hi Marta / Tim,

Thanks for the call yesterday. Attached are the redlines for the MSA and Order Form (see files: Acme_MSA_redlines_20270608.docx, OrderForm_Acme_v3_redline.pdf). The items we need to resolve before we can route to signature are primarily DPA/SCCs delegation and the signature-gating point on the Order Form (Section 4.2). High level asks: 

- Confirm whether Acme can accept Processor role for non-EU environments while Redwood remains Controller for EU-origin data, and whether the proposed SCCs delegation matrix (attached) is acceptable.
- We request that the DPA exhibit be countersigned (not just agreed in redlines) before the Order Form is executed — this is our standard signature gate for accounts with cross-border processing.
- If Acme requires Order Form execution first for procurement/PO purposes, we can accept a conditional acceptance with a short-term interim hosting restriction (EU-only processing for first 90 days) and a hard deadline to finalize the DPA exhibit.

Jonas and Laura are on copy to coordinate legal and procurement sequencing. Please review and flag the top 3 items you want to discuss live — I can book a quick 30m tomorrow.

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (650) 555-0132

Attachments: Acme_MSA_redlines_20270608.docx (document), OrderForm_Acme_v3_redline.pdf (pdf), SCCs-delegation-matrix.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@acmeco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Tim Reed <tim.reed@acmeco.com>
Cc: AcmeCo Legal <legal@acmeco.com>
Date: Wed, 9 Jun 2027 13:42:00 +0200
Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form redlines — DPA/SCCs delegation and signature gate

Ben — thanks for the files and the clear asks. Reading through the attachments this morning. A couple quick clarifications from our side: 

1) Acme's standard position is to have the Order Form executed to trigger PO and budget flows; we then sign ancillary exhibits afterward. Is the conditional Order Form approach (EU-only processing for 90 days) something Redwood is comfortable enforcing contractually (we need concrete language).

2) On roles: our legal team prefers a split approach where Acme is Controller for customer data sets we ingest (customer-provided PII) and Redwood is Controller of service-level telemetry and model-derived outputs. Can you confirm how Redwood maps these buckets in the DPA?

3) SCCs delegation matrix — we see three delegation lines marked as "Customer Delegated". Our legal team will pull this into their redline and propose alternative language for Exhibit B.

We can do a 30m sync tomorrow at 10:00 CET (09:00 UTC) if that works. Copying our in-house counsel for the redline pass.

Best,
Marta Lopez
Head of Legal Ops, AcmeCo
marta.lopez@acmeco.com

On Wed, Jun 9, 2027 at 09:15 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Marta / Tim,
> 
> Thanks for the call yesterday. Attached are the redlines for the MSA and Order Form (see files...).

From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
To: Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@acmeco.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 08:04:00 +0200
Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form redlines — DPA/SCCs delegation and signature gate

Thanks Marta — quick technical/legal clarity from Redwood (Jonas, Legal Ops lead):

- Bucket mapping: we propose the following simple division in the DPA: (i) Customer-supplied PII and uploaded datasets = Controller (Acme) / Processor (Redwood); (ii) Model outputs that are direct transformations of customer data = Processor for Redwood unless customer marks outputs as product-specific retained ownership; (iii) Service-level telemetry / billing logs = Controller (Redwood). We'll convert this into short exhibit language and attach an example mapping table to the DPA.

- Signature gate: the conditional Order Form approach is acceptable provided we put in two controls: (a) a 90-day interim hosting restriction that is auditable (we will limit all EU workloads to EU-hosted capacity), and (b) an explicit milestone with consequences (Order Form void or automatic suspension of new provisioning) if the DPA exhibit is not signed by day 90. We will draft the clause and remove ambiguity about enforcement.

- SCCs delegation: Acme's suggested edits to Exhibit B are OK in principle; we need to show the exact delegation map to our internal compliance team for KMS/access control mapping. If Acme requires Acme-managed keying (bring-your-own-key / HSM), we need to scope operational changes and a cost addendum — procurement should be aware.

Action items from Redwood:
1) Draft a short exhibit mapping buckets and insert the signature-gate clause (Jonas to provide by EOD today).
2) Laura to confirm procurement wording for PO alignment and cost addendum triggers.

Jonas Weber
Head of Legal Operations | Redwood Inference
jonas.weber@redwood.ai

> Ben — thanks for the files and the clear asks...


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@acmeco.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 12:29:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form redlines — DPA/SCCs delegation and signature gate

Marta / Team — a couple procurement/process notes from my side (Laura, Head of Revenue Operations):

- If Acme needs the Order Form signed to satisfy internal PO gating, Redwood can accept the Order Form signature before the DPA exhibit is fully executed only under a signed "Conditional Execution" rider (we will provide language). The rider will: (1) require Acme to finalize the DPA exhibit within 90 days; (2) require Acme to confirm in writing that processing for EU-subject data will be limited to EU-region hosts until the DPA is executed; (3) include an escrowed holdback for onboarding credits if the DPA misses the milestone.

- Procurement note: BYOK/HSM options will incur a one-time validation fee plus a monthly key-management surcharge; we'll include numbers in the cost addendum. Please flag if a procurement contact needs a line-itemized estimate for the FY28 budget slot.

- For speed, if legal and compliance want, we can run a parallel doc-sign flow: sign the conditional Order Form + rider now via DocuSign, and run the DPA exhibit redline in parallel with a separate DocuSign envelope. That maintains procurement momentum while protecting both parties.

I will prepare the rider and the preliminary cost addendum draft once Jonas posts the signature-gate clause.

Regards,
Laura Bennett
Head of Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

From: Marco Salazar <marco.salazar@medigen.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: security@medigen.com
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700

Hi Ava,

title: Final MAP alignment, signer windows, and start-date (go/no-go)

From: Amiya Shah <amiya.shah@orbitai.com>

Cc: Jason Silver <j.silver@orbitai.com>, contracts@orbitai.com
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Final MAP: go/no-go and signer availability

Hi Irene,

Thanks for the walkthrough yesterday — Orbit is ready to lock down the mutual action plan and confirm the final signature timing so we can hit a July 1st production start. Two asks from our side before we commit: 1) a short list of the final signers and their available signing windows (we have limited exec availability next week), and 2) the final DocuSign packet (we prefer to receive one consolidated envelope).

I've attached our internal acceptance checklist for the evaluation (EvaluationPlan_OrbitAI.pdf) and a proposed delivery cadence. We're comfortable with a canary for the first two weeks if the SLA exhibits are agreed.

Proposed high-level timeline:
- Jun 18: Orbit final internal approval (legal + procurement)
- Jun 19–21: DocuSign circulation
- Jun 22: Redwood sign-off and countersign
- Jun 29: Soft launch (canary)

Can you share who will be listed as the Redwood signers and whether you can expedite the DocuSign?

Thanks,
Amiya
--
Amiya Shah
Head of Integrations, OrbitAI
amiya.shah@orbitai.com
+1 415 555 0123

Attachment: EvaluationPlan_OrbitAI.pdf (PDF, 480KB)

Quick answers and asks from our side:
- Proposed Redwood signers: Irene Choi (VP Partnerships) and Marissa Cole (Head of Legal). Both have limited windows the week of Jun 21–25: morning slots (9:00–11:30 PT) work best.
- We can prepare the DocuSign envelope once Orbit confirms the exact signer names and who needs to be CC'd for audit.
- Ben: please confirm acceptance gates for canary.
- Kimberly: procurement will need a PO and billing contact; can Orbit confirm billing email and payment term preference (net 30 vs net 45)?

I've attached the latest MAP-v3.xlsx (timeline + action owners) — note the updated rollback triggers under Gate 2.

If Orbit can commit to sign by Jun 19–21 we can have the envelope out the same day and still meet a Jun 29 soft launch.

Best,
Irene

--
Irene Choi
VP Partnerships, Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwood.ai

Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 13:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Final MAP: go/no-go and signer availability

Quick technical gating note from CS/ops:
- Canary scope: 2 clusters (east/west) with traffic ramp to 10% over 48 hours, monitoring for latency/regression.
- Acceptance criteria for lifting canary: error rate <0.5% and 95th percentile latency < 420ms over a 24h window.
- Rollback plan: automatic routing to baseline model + pause of batch jobs; we will run a smoke test checklist before canary opens.

I'm comfortable with the Jun 29 soft launch if Orbit signs by Jun 21 and we complete preflight by Jun 25. If Orbit needs an earlier start, we can do a one-day preflight on Jun 28 and still hold the soft launch Jun 29.

Attaching SLA-Exhibit-Draft.docx for Marissa to review proposed availability/credits language.

Ben
--
Ben Carter
Director, Customer Success, Redwood Inference
