title: Routed burst buffer proposal for multi-deal onboarding
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Samantha Liu <samantha.liu@nova-ai.com>
Cc: 
Date: Wed, 02 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Routed burst buffer proposal for multi-deal onboarding

Team / Samantha,

Quick note to start the thread on capacity sizing for Nova's rollout (deal NOVA-2027-09-EX3). They expect a high-concurrency batch window during product launch weeks, plus steady inference volume afterward. Rather than grant a large static dedicated pool in each region, I'm proposing a routed burst buffer model: a small reserved pool per region (baseline commit) + a centrally routed burst pool that can spill across US/EU/APAC with enforced fallback priorities and per-deal token budgets. Advantages: lower committed spend, simpler burst coordination, and faster ramp for the initial 90 days.

Initial asks (one-pagers):
- Marissa: can you run 3 sizing scenarios (conservative / expected / worst) and attach the delta to the deal model?
- Hannah: what are the SLA gating conditions we need to accept for cross-region spill? Any legal/compliance flags in APAC we should surface?
- Irene: ops feasibility — how quickly can the routing policy be implemented (switch + rollout) and what monitoring/alerts do we need?

Samantha, for context I attached the traffic profile you shared last week (nova_traffic_profile_may2027.csv). If you can confirm peak concurrency and % of tokens that are latency-sensitive vs batch, that will help the scenarios.

Proposed timeline: sizing playbook by EoD Friday, ops feasibility by next Wednesday, final ask to finance for temporary burst credit next Thursday.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments: nova_traffic_profile_may2027.csv


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Samantha Liu <samantha.liu@nova-ai.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Jun 2027 11:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Routed burst buffer proposal for multi-deal onboarding

Ben / all,

Thanks — I ran three scenarios and summarized them in deal_capacity_model_v1.xlsx (attached). High level:

- Conservative: Reserve minimal regional pools (US: 8 A100 equiv, EU: 6, APAC: 4) + central burst pool 12. Cost to Nova (monthly): projected $78k.
- Expected: Regional baseline (US: 16, EU: 12, APAC: 8) + central burst 24. Cost: $135k/mo.
- Worst: Regional baseline (US: 28, EU: 20, APAC: 12) + central burst 48. Cost: $240k/mo.

I added a per-deal token cap for the central burst to protect other customers and a suggested ramp schedule (weeks 0-4, 5-8, 9-12). The sheet includes per-region latency percentiles and suggested quantization profiles for batch windows.

Notes for finance: the central burst will require a one-time temporary credit (we can structure as a refundable buffer) and a short SLA carve-out while routing policies are validated.

I attached: deal_capacity_model_v1.xlsx

—Marissa
Marissa Cole
Senior Partnerships Manager | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

On Wed, Jun 2, 2027 at 09:12 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team / Samantha,
>
> Quick note to start the thread on capacity sizing for Nova's rollout (deal NOVA-2027-09-EX3). ...


From: Samantha Liu <samantha.liu@nova-ai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 02 Jun 2027 13:36:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Routed burst buffer proposal for multi-deal onboarding

Hi Ben / Marissa,

Thanks for the quick turnaround. A few clarifications from Nova's side:
- Peak concurrency estimate: 18k concurrent sessions for a 90-minute launch window, tapering to 2–4k steady.
- Latency-sensitive traffic: ~62% of tokens during launch (these are in-product interactive calls). The rest can be batched with 3–5s latency.
- APAC constraint: we cannot route certain PII-involving calls outside Singapore due to partner data residency rules. Those will need to remain in APAC baseline pool.

We reviewed the model and the expected scenario looks closest to our appetite. We are comfortable with a per-deal central burst cap if you can guarantee 95th percentile latency SLAs for the latency-sensitive subset when routed to a non-origin region.

I've attached a cleaned traffic export so Marissa can validate numbers: nova_traffic_profile_may2027.csv

Samantha
Samantha Liu
Head of Platform | Nova AI
samantha.liu@nova-ai.com

On Wed, Jun 2, 2027 at 11:04 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Ben / all,
>
> Thanks — I ran three scenarios ...


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Samantha Liu <samantha.liu@nova-ai.com>
Date: Thu, 03 Jun 2027 09:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Routed burst buffer proposal for multi-deal onboarding

All,

Quick compliance/SLA notes:
- APAC data residency: agree with Samantha — any requests with PII flagged must stay in Singapore region. We'll mark those traffic classes and exclude them from cross-region spill.
- SLA exception: to allow cross-region routing for latency-sensitive traffic during launch we need an executive SLA carve-out (max 72-hour evaluation window) and an incident runbook documenting fallback to local baseline pools. I sketched a one-page SLA_exception_summary.pdf (attached) that lists thresholds, rollback triggers, and who authorizes the carve-out.
- Legal will require explicit consent language from Nova for cross-border fallback during the ramp. Please coordinate with their procurement.

Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Head of Customer Reliability | Redwood Inference

On Wed, Jun 2, 2027 at 01:36 PM Samantha Liu <samantha.liu@nova-ai.com> wrote:
> Hi Ben / Marissa,
> ...


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Samantha Liu <samantha.liu@nova-ai.com>
Date: Thu, 03 Jun 2027 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Routed burst buffer proposal for multi-deal onboarding

Ben / team,

Ops feasibility notes:
- Routing policy: implementable in two sprints. Sprint 1 (policy + metrics): create central routing service with per-deal token buckets and priority ladder (1) local baseline, (2) region sibling, (3) central burst. Sprint 2 (hardening): KV cache affinity tweaks and rollback tooling.
- Monitoring: we need 95/99 latency percentiles for the latency-sensitive class, dropped token counts, and cross-region egress counters. Suggested alert thresholds are in routed_buffer_model_v2.xlsx (attached).
- Time to deploy: minimum 10 business days for staged rollout if we reuse current runtime hooks. If we need a hotfix to runtime kernel selection, add 5 business days.

I've attached routed_buffer_model_v2.xlsx which includes per-region capacity impact and a mock routing policy.

Irene Choi
Sr. Product Manager, Serving Runtime | Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwood.ai

On Thu, Jun 3, 2027 at 09:28 AM Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai> wrote:
> All,
> ...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Samantha Liu <samantha.liu@nova-ai.com>, Peter Ng <peter.ng@cloudregion.com>
Date: Fri, 04 Jun 2027 16:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Routed burst buffer proposal for multi-deal onboarding

Thanks — good progress. Final next steps:
1) Marissa — finalize the expected scenario numbers and send a short note to finance with the refundable burst credit ask. Please copy legal on the APAC residency carve-out language.
2) Irene — schedule the two-sprint implementation and share a deployment timetable. We'll need an ops readiness sign-off before launch weekend.
3) Hannah — finalize the SLA_exception_summary.pdf and confirm who signs the carve-out on our side.
4) Samantha — please confirm acceptance of the expected scenario and the APAC carve-out by EoD Monday; we will not start cross-region routing without explicit authorization.

Peter (CloudRegion) — looping you in: we may need temporary burst capacity on your Singapore edge for the APAC baseline. Can you confirm pool availability for the week of the launch (target week 26)?

I'll open a short DocuSign for the temporary credit once Marissa confirms numbers. Link will be posted to the Drive folder and HubSpot deal.

Cheers,
Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: deal_capacity_model_v1.xlsx, SLA_exception_summary.pdf, routed_buffer_model_v2.xlsx
